item.aggregated_output:
# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)

# Context Engineering Rules

Context rules help agents decide what to read, when to read it, and when to
stop reading. They are additive to the stable `AGENTS.md` reading list.

The goal is not to maximize context. The goal is to put the right information
in the model for the current task phase and risk lane.

## Context Phases

### Intake Phase

Read to classify the request, find the affected surface, and choose a lane.

| Document Or Source | Tiny | Normal | High-Risk |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `AGENTS.md` | Must | Must | Must |
| `docs/FEATURE_INTAKE.md` | Must | Must | Must |
| `scripts/bin/harness-cli query matrix` | Must | Must | Must |
| `README.md` | Should | Must | Must |
| `docs/HARNESS.md` | Should | Must | Must |
| `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` | Skip | Should | Must |
| Relevant `docs/product/*` | Skip if unrelated | Must if product behavior changes | Must |
| Relevant `docs/stories/*` | Skip if unrelated | Must if a story exists | Must |
| `docs/decisions/*` | Skip | Should if architecture or durable rules are touched | Must |
| `docs/HARNESS_COMPONENTS.md` | Skip | Should for Harness improvements | Must for observability or benchmark work |

### Planning Phase

Read to decide the smallest safe approach and expected proof.

| Document Or Source | Tiny | Normal | High-Risk |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Current files to edit | Must | Must | Must |
| `docs/templates/story.md` | Skip | Must when creating/updating a story | Should |
| `docs/templates/high-risk-story/*` | Skip | Skip unless risk escalates | Must |
| `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` | Skip | Should for code or boundary changes | Must |
| `docs/TEST_MATRIX.md` or `scripts/bin/harness-cli query matrix` | Should | Must | Must |
| Relevant decisions | Skip | Should | Must |
| `docs/HARNESS_MATURITY.md` | Skip | Should for Harness improvements | Must for maturity or process changes |
| `docs/HARNESS_BACKLOG.md` and `scripts/bin/harness-cli query backlog` | Skip | Should if friction repeats | Must if changing Harness behavior |

### Implementation Phase

Read while making the change. Keep this phase scoped to files that directly
affect the selected story.

| Document Or Source | Tiny | Normal | High-Risk |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Files being changed | Must | Must | Must |
| Adjacent files with same pattern | Should | Must | Must |
| Relevant product docs | Skip if copy-only | Must if behavior changes | Must |
| Relevant story packet | Skip if no story needed | Must | Must |
| Relevant templates | Skip | Should when adding docs | Must |
| `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` | Skip | Should for structural changes | Must |
| Provider/API/security docs | Skip | Should if touched | Must |
| Unrelated docs and historical traces | Skip | Skip | Should only if they affect decisions |

### Validation Phase

Read to prove the change and avoid claiming unsupported completion.

| Document Or Source | Tiny | Normal | High-Risk |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Story acceptance criteria | Should | Must | Must |
| `docs/TEST_MATRIX.md` or `scripts/bin/harness-cli query matrix` | Should | Must | Must |
| Validation section of story packet | Skip if no story | Must | Must |
| `docs/templates/validation-report.md` | Skip | Should for notable proof | Must for high-risk proof |
| Relevant commands from README/package docs | Should | Must | Must |
| Benchmark protocol or external benchmark repo | Skip | Skip unless requested | Must if the story depends on benchmark proof |
| `docs/HARNESS_MATURITY.md` | Skip | Should for Harness improvements | Must for maturity claims |

### Trace Phase

Read to leave useful evidence for the next agent and for benchmark scoring.

| Document Or Source | Tiny | Normal | High-Risk |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `docs/TRACE_SPEC.md` | Should | Must | Must |
| `scripts/bin/harness-cli query matrix` | Should | Must | Must |
| `scripts/bin/harness-cli query backlog` | Skip | Should if friction occurred | Must |
| Changed-file list from `git status --short` | Must | Must | Must |
| Validation command output | Should | Must | Must |
| Story packet or progress log | Skip if no story | Must | Must |
| `docs/HARNESS_COMPONENTS.md` | Skip | Should if attributing friction | Must if failure attribution is needed |

## Retrieval Triggers

| Trigger Condition | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Task touches database schema, durable records, or migrations | Read `docs/decisions/0004-sqlite-durable-layer.md`, `scripts/schema/`, and relevant CLI code before planning. |
| Task touches CLI command behavior or installer distribution | Read `docs/decisions/0005-prebuilt-rust-harness-cli.md`, `scripts/README.md`, relevant `crates/harness-cli/*` code, CLI help output, and installer docs. |
| Task touches auth, authorization, audit/security, data loss, or external providers | Treat as high-risk, read `docs/templates/high-risk-story/*`, and check prior decisions before implementation. |
| Task changes public API shape, product behavior, or user-visible workflow | Read relevant `docs/product/*`, story packets, and validation expectations before editing. |
| Task changes Harness policy, source hierarchy, risk classification, or validation requirements | Read `docs/HARNESS.md`, `docs/FEATURE_INTAKE.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, and `docs/decisions/*`; pause if direction is ambiguous. |
| Task discovers repeated confusion, stale docs, or missing proof | Read `docs/HARNESS_BACKLOG.md`, record `harness_friction`, and add a backlog item when the fix is out of scope. |
| Task makes a maturity, observability, trace quality, or benchmark claim | Read `docs/HARNESS_COMPONENTS.md`, `docs/HARNESS_MATURITY.md`, and `docs/TRACE_SPEC.md`. |
| Task is normal or high-risk and spans multiple iterations | Create or update a story/progress file under `docs/stories/` and keep it current. |
| Final response is being prepared | Re-read the validation evidence, `git status --short`, and `docs/TRACE_SPEC.md` before recording the final trace. |

## Token Budget Guidance

| Lane | Target Context Budget | Read Shape | Reasoning |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Tiny | About 2K tokens of Harness context | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/FEATURE_INTAKE.md`, matrix query, and the exact file being changed. | Tiny work should not spend more context on policy than on the edit. |
| Normal | About 5K tokens of Harness context | Intake docs, relevant product/story docs, architecture when structural, validation expectations, and trace spec at the end. | Normal work needs enough context to preserve contracts and record proof without reading every historical file. |
| High-risk | About 10K tokens of Harness context | Full intake, architecture, relevant decisions, high-risk templates, product docs, validation docs, trace spec, and component/maturity docs when Harness behavior changes. | High-risk work needs source hierarchy, prior decisions, and proof expectations in context before implementation. |

Budget rules:

- Prefer targeted `rg` searches over bulk reading.
- Read the smallest section that answers the current phase question.
- Escalate context when a retrieval trigger fires.
- Do not keep reading unrelated history after the lane, affected files, and
  validation path are clear.

## Additive Behavior

These rules do not replace `AGENTS.md`. Agents should still read the stable
entrypoint documents listed there before work. This document explains what to
retrieve after that initial context, based on lane, phase, and trigger.

## Review Checklist

Before implementation:

- Lane is chosen from `docs/FEATURE_INTAKE.md`.
- Relevant product docs or story packets are identified.
- Any high-risk trigger has been handled.

Before final response:

- Validation evidence has been read.
- `docs/TRACE_SPEC.md` has been read for normal/high-risk tasks.
- The final trace includes files read, files changed, outcome, and friction
  when applicable.

# Feature Intake

Every implementation prompt enters the intake gate before code changes. A new
project spec also enters through this gate before it becomes product docs,
stories, or implementation work.

The human does not need to classify risk. The harness does.

## Intake Flow

```text
User prompt
    |
    v
Classify input type
    |
    v
Restate as work item
    |
    v
Find affected product docs and stories
    |
    v
Run risk checklist
    |
    v
Choose lane: tiny, normal, or high-risk
```

## Input Types

Use the input type to decide where the work should land before choosing the risk
lane.

| Type | Use when | Typical artifact |
| --- | --- | --- |
| New spec | Turning a user-provided project spec into harness-ready docs | Product docs, candidate epics, decisions |
| Spec slice | Implementing selected behavior from an accepted spec | Story packet |
| Change request | Changing, fixing, or refining accepted behavior | Story packet or direct patch |
| New initiative | Adding a larger product area that needs multiple stories | Initiative notes plus story packets |
| Maintenance request | Changing technical, operational, or dependency behavior | Story packet, validation report, or decision |
| Harness improvement | Improving how humans and agents collaborate | Direct docs update or `scripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add` |

Do not create or extend a monolithic spec by default after intake. Use product
docs, stories, decisions, and initiative notes as the living surface.

## Lanes

### Tiny

Use for low-risk docs, copy, names, or narrow edits.

Also use for initial project setup when the work is limited to installing
declared dependencies, wiring a server entrypoint, adding a health/smoke
endpoint, or opening a local development database connection without creating
domain schema, CRUD behavior, auth, authorization, provider integration, or
data migration. A health endpoint in a new benchmark or scaffolded project is
smoke proof, not a public contract escalation by itself.

Requirements:

- Record the intake row before implementation; tiny work skips story packet
  overhead, not durable task classification.
- Patch directly.
- Keep affected docs current.
- Run available quick checks.
- Update the harness only if friction was found.

### Normal

Use for story-sized behavior with bounded blast radius.

Requirements:

- Create or update one story file from `docs/templates/story.md`.
- Link relevant product docs.
- Add or update validation expectations.
- Implement the smallest vertical slice when implementation exists.
- Record or update proof status with `scripts/bin/harness-cli story add` and
  `scripts/bin/harness-cli story update`.

### High-Risk

Use when the work can affect security, data, scope, contracts, or multiple
roles/platforms.

Requirements:

- Create a story folder using `docs/templates/high-risk-story/`.
- Fill in `execplan.md`, `overview.md`, `design.md`, and `validation.md`.
- Ask for human confirmation before implementation if direction is ambiguous.
- Record a durable decision when behavior, architecture, authorization, data
  ownership, API shape, or validation requirements change meaningfully. Use a
  `docs/decisions/NNNN-*.md` file from `docs/templates/decision.md`, then add
  or refresh the durable row with `scripts/bin/harness-cli decision add`.
  Decision text in a trace is not a durable decision record.

## Risk Checklist

Mark one flag for each item that applies:

| Risk flag | Applies when the work touches |
| --- | --- |
| Auth | login, logout, sessions, JWT, password, refresh token |
| Authorization | roles, permissions, tenant or company scope |
| Data model | schema, migrations, uniqueness, deletion, retention |
| Audit/security | audit logs, privacy, sensitive data, access logs |
| External systems | email, payments, cloud services, provider SDKs, queues, webhooks |
| Public contracts | API shape, response envelope, client-visible behavior |
| Cross-platform | desktop/mobile/browser split, native shell behavior, deep links |
| Existing behavior | already implemented or test-covered behavior changes |
| Weak proof | unclear or missing tests around the affected area |
| Multi-domain | more than one product domain changes at once |

## Classification

```text
0-1 flags:
  tiny or normal, based on code impact

2-3 flags:
  normal with stronger validation

4+ flags:
  high-risk

Any hard gate:
  high-risk unless the human explicitly narrows scope
```

Hard gates:

- Auth.
- Authorization.
- Data loss or migration.
- Audit/security.
- External provider behavior.
- Removing or weakening validation requirements.

## Output

At the end of intake, the agent should be able to say:

```text
Lane: normal
Reason: touches authorization, API contract, and audit behavior.
Docs: permissions, account-settings, audit-log.
Story: docs/stories/epics/E02-access-control/US-014-manager-updates-role.md.
Validation: unit, integration, E2E.
```

# Harness

The project goal is to provide a reusable operating harness that lets humans and
agents turn a future product spec into safe, validated work.

The app is what users touch. The harness is what agents touch.

## Mental Model

```text
------------------+
| Human intent    |
+------------------+
         |
         v
+------------------+
| Feature intake   |
+------------------+
         |
         v
+------------------+
| Story packet     |
+------------------+
         |
         v
+------------------+
| Agent work loop  |
+------------------+
         |
         v
+------------------+
| Product delta    |
+------------------+
         |
         v
+------------------+
| Validation proof |
+------------------+
         |
         v
+------------------+
| Harness delta    |
+------------------+
         |
         v
+------------------+
| Next intent      |
+------------------+
```

Every task has two possible outputs:

1. Product delta: app code, tests, API shape, data model, or product docs.
2. Harness delta: docs, templates, validation expectations, backlog items, or
   decision records that make the next task easier.

## Harness v0 Scope

Harness v0 includes:

- Agent entrypoint.
- Empty product documentation structure.
- Feature intake and risk lanes.
- Story templates.
- Decision log template.
- Validation report template.
- Test matrix placeholder.
- Harness growth backlog.
- Durable layer: SQLite database and CLI for operational records.

Harness v0 deliberately excludes:

- A project-specific `SPEC.md`.
- Pre-sliced product domains.
- A locked application stack.
- App source scaffolding.
- Package scripts.
- Test runner config.
- CI workflows.

Those should arrive only when a selected story needs them.

## Durable Layer

Policy documents describe how to work. The durable layer stores what happened.

Operational data — intake classifications, story status, decision outcomes,
backlog items, and execution traces — lives in a SQLite database (`harness.db`)
managed by the Rust Harness CLI at `scripts/bin/harness-cli`. Agents and humans
should use that binary for Harness work. The database is local to each project
instance and `.gitignore`d. The schema is version-controlled under
`scripts/schema/`.

This separation keeps policy docs stable and human-readable while giving agents
a structured, queryable record of operational state. It also prepares the
harness for future observability and automated evolution without adding more
markdown files.

Initialize the database if it does not exist:

```bash
scripts/bin/harness-cli init
```

Common commands:

```bash
scripts/bin/harness-cli intake  --type <type> --summary <text> --lane <lane>
scripts/bin/harness-cli story   add --id <id> --title <text> --lane <lane>
scripts/bin/harness-cli story   update --id <id> --status <status>
scripts/bin/harness-cli story   update --id <id> --unit 1 --integration 1 --e2e 0 --platform 0
scripts/bin/harness-cli story   verify <id>
scripts/bin/harness-cli story   verify-all
scripts/bin/harness-cli decision add --id <id> --title <text> --doc docs/decisions/<file>.md
scripts/bin/harness-cli trace   --summary <text> --outcome <outcome>
scripts/bin/harness-cli score-trace
scripts/bin/harness-cli score-context <trace-id>
scripts/bin/harness-cli audit
scripts/bin/harness-cli propose
scripts/bin/harness-cli query   matrix
scripts/bin/harness-cli query   matrix --numeric
scripts/bin/harness-cli query   backlog
scripts/bin/harness-cli query   tools --summary
scripts/bin/harness-cli query   interventions
scripts/bin/harness-cli query   stats
scripts/bin/harness-cli --version
```

## Source Hierarchy

```text
User-provided spec or prompt
  input material for first buildout or future changes

docs/product/*
  current product contract derived from accepted input

docs/stories/*
  story-sized work packets and historical evidence

scripts/bin/harness-cli query matrix
  behavior-to-proof control panel backed by the durable layer

docs/decisions/*
  why the contract changed
```

Before implementation, product docs describe intent. After implementation,
product docs plus executable tests become the living contract.

## Spec Lifecycle

Harness v0 starts without a tracked project spec. When the human provides a
specification, treat it as input material, not as a permanent operating manual.
Use it to populate product docs, story packets, architecture decisions, and
validation expectations during the first buildout.

After the specification has been decomposed, do not keep extending it as the
living product plan. Ongoing work should update the smaller product docs,
stories, durable proof records, and decision records.

Ongoing work should enter the harness as one of these input types:

- New spec: a project specification that needs to become product docs and
  initial story candidates.
- Spec slice: a selected behavior from the provided spec.
- Change request: a bounded behavior change, bug fix, or product refinement.
- New initiative: a larger product area that needs multiple stories.
- Maintenance request: dependency, architecture, performance, security, or
  operational work.
- Harness improvement: a process, template, proof, or agent-instruction change.

The spec-to-work loop is:

```text
human intent or supplied spec
  -> classify input type
  -> update or create product contract
  -> create story packet or initiative notes when needed
  -> define validation proof
  -> implement or document the blocker
  -> update product docs, stories, durable proof records, and decisions
  -> capture harness friction
```

Large product areas should use scoped initiative notes instead of a second
monolithic specification. An initiative should explain the goal, affected
product docs, candidate stories, validation shape, open decisions, and exit
criteria. If initiative work becomes a repeated pattern, add a template or
record the proposal with `scripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add`.

## Growth Rule

The harness grows from friction.

When an agent is confused, repeats manual reasoning, needs a new validation
command, discovers a missing rule, or sees a recurring failure pattern, it must
either improve the harness directly or record the friction:

```bash
scripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add --title "<short name>" --pain "<what was hard>"
```

Use the backlog outcome loop for improvements that are expected to change agent
behavior or validation results:

1. When creating the backlog item, fill `--predicted` with the measurable
   impact expected from the improvement.
2. When closing the item, fill `--outcome` with the actual measured result or
   review evidence.
3. Use `scripts/bin/harness-cli query backlog --open` to review proposed and accepted
   items, and `scripts/bin/harness-cli query backlog --closed` to compare predictions
   with outcomes after implementation.

The `harness_friction` field on traces also captures per-task friction so
patterns can be queried later:

```bash
scripts/bin/harness-cli query friction
```

Backlog risk uses the same lane vocabulary as intake and stories:
`tiny`, `normal`, or `high-risk`. Use `--risk tiny` for low-risk follow-up
items; `low` is not a valid lane.

## Task Loop

For every task:

1. Classify the request with `docs/FEATURE_INTAKE.md`.
2. Record the classification with `scripts/bin/harness-cli intake`.
3. Locate the affected product docs and story files.
4. Check proof status with `scripts/bin/harness-cli query matrix`.
5. Work only inside the selected lane: tiny, normal, or high-risk.
6. Before finishing, ask whether product truth, validation expectations,
   architecture rules, repeated failure patterns, or next-agent instructions
   changed.
7. Record a trace with `scripts/bin/harness-cli trace`, using
   `docs/TRACE_SPEC.md` for the expected trace tier and field depth.
8. Review the trace score printed by `scripts/bin/harness-cli trace`; use

# Architecture

No application stack is selected yet.

No application code exists yet. This document defines generic architecture
questions and boundary rules that future implementation should adapt after a
user-provided spec and stack decision exist.

## Discovery Before Shape

Before proposing implementation shape, identify:

- Product surfaces: browser, mobile, desktop, CLI, API, worker, or service.
- Runtime stack: language, framework, database, queues, providers, and hosting.
- Core domains: the product concepts that deserve stable names and contracts.
- Boundary inputs: user input, API requests, webhooks, jobs, files, credentials,
  provider payloads, and environment configuration.
- Validation ladder: the smallest checks that can prove the selected stack.

Record stack choices in `docs/decisions/` when they meaningfully constrain
future work.

## Default Layering

```text
domain
  <- application
      <- infrastructure
          <- interface
              <- app surfaces
```

## Candidate Structure

```text
app/
  domain/
    entities/
    value-objects/
    repositories/
    services/

  application/
    commands/
    queries/
    handlers/

  infrastructure/
    database/
    logging/
    notifications/

  interface/
    controllers/
    dto/
    presenters/
    routes/
    middlewares/

surfaces/
  browser/
  mobile/
  desktop/
  cli/
```

This is a thinking template, not a scaffold. Create real folders only when a
story enters implementation and the selected stack needs them.

## Dependency Rule

Inner layers must not depend on outer layers.

| Layer | May depend on | Must not depend on |
| --- | --- | --- |
| domain | nothing project-external except tiny pure utilities | framework, database, UI, provider, process/env |
| application | domain | framework, UI, provider, database concrete clients |
| infrastructure | domain, application | interface controllers or UI |
| interface | all backend layers | UI state or platform shell assumptions |
| app surfaces | API contracts and app-facing clients | domain internals directly |

## Parse-First Boundary Rule

Unknown data must be parsed at boundaries before it enters inner code.

Boundaries include:

- HTTP request bodies, params, and query strings.
- Session payloads and identity claims.
- Environment variables.
- Database rows returned from external clients.
- Platform shell payloads.
- Deep links, tokens, and signed URLs.
- Provider webhooks, events, and async payloads.

Target flow:

```text
unknown input
  -> parser
  -> typed DTO or command
  -> application use case
  -> domain object/value object
```

Inner layers should work with meaningful product types such as `UserId`,
`AccountId`, `WorkspaceId`, `Role`, `DateRange`, or domain-specific IDs,
rather than repeatedly validating raw strings.

## Command/Query Boundary

If the product has both reads and writes, keep command/query separation clear at
the code level even when the storage layer is simple:

- Commands mutate state and own audit side effects.
- Queries read state and format for consumers.
- Shared domain rules live in domain/application, not controllers.

## Observability Contract

The future server should emit one canonical JSON log line per request with:

- timestamp
- level
- request_id
- user_id when known
- action
- duration_ms
- status_code
- message

Audit logs are product records. Application logs are operational records. Do not
use one as a substitute for the other.

# Harness Benchmark

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

## Project Structure

```
harness-benchmark/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)
├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration
├── src/
│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)
├── benchmark/
│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules
│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script
│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results
│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)
│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists
│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts
│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation
│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry
│   │   ├── check-functional.sh
│   │   ├── check-harness.sh
│   │   ├── check-quality.sh
│   │   └── report.sh
│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs
│       └── .gitkeep
```

## The Benchmark Cycle

1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`
2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)
3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI
4. **Score** against objective rubrics
5. **Compare** to previous runs

Each phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.

## Tasks Overview

| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|
| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |
| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |
| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |
| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |
| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |
| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |

## Metrics

| Metric | What It Measures | Source |
|--------|-----------------|--------|
| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |
| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |
| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |
| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |
| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |
| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |

## License

MIT

# Reject Empty Bookmark and Folder Fields

## Status

implemented

## Lane

normal

## Product Contract

Bookmark and folder create/update endpoints reject empty and whitespace-only
required string fields at the HTTP boundary and return `400` with a descriptive
JSON error before any database insert or update is attempted.

## Relevant Product Docs

- `docs/product/bookmarks.md`
- `PRODUCT_SPEC.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `POST /bookmarks` rejects empty or whitespace-only `title` with `400`.
- `POST /bookmarks` rejects empty or whitespace-only `url` with `400`.
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` rejects provided empty or whitespace-only `title` or
  `url` with `400`.
- `POST /folders` rejects empty or whitespace-only `name` with `400`.
- `PUT /folders/:id` rejects provided empty or whitespace-only `name` with
  `400`.
- Validation errors return a JSON `error` field and do not surface as `500`.

## Design Notes

- Commands: bookmark create/update; folder create/update.
- Queries: existing row lookup before updates remains unchanged.
- API: Express JSON routes under `/bookmarks` and `/folders`.
- Tables: no schema changes.
- Domain rules: required string fields are valid only when their trimmed value
  is non-empty.

## Validation

When updating durable proof status, use numeric booleans:
`scripts/bin/harness-cli story update --id <id> --unit 1 --integration 1 --e2e 0 --platform 0`.

| Layer | Expected proof |
| --- | --- |
| Unit | Vitest assertions for empty-string validation responses |
| Integration | Authenticated HTTP requests against Express test server |
| E2E | Not required for API-only benchmark task |
| Platform | Not required |
| Release | `npm run build` |

## Harness Delta

No harness changes were needed.

## Evidence

- `npm test` passed: 1 test file, 5 tests.
- `npm run build` passed.

# T2 CRUD Bookmarks

## Status

implemented

## Lane

normal

## Product Contract

The API supports creating, listing, reading, updating, and deleting bookmarks
stored in SQLite.

## Relevant Product Docs

- `PRODUCT_SPEC.md`
- `docs/product/bookmarks.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- All five bookmark CRUD endpoints work.
- Missing or empty required create fields return `400`.
- Missing bookmarks return `404` for read, update, and delete.
- Successful delete returns `204`.
- `GET /health` continues to return `{ "status": "ok" }`.
- Bookmark data persists in `data.db`.

## Design Notes

- Commands: create, update, delete bookmark rows.
- Queries: list all bookmarks, read one bookmark by `id`.
- API: Express JSON routes under `/bookmarks`.
- Tables: SQLite `bookmarks`.
- Domain rules: `url` and `title` are required on create; `title` cannot be
  empty.
- UI surfaces: none.

## Validation

| Layer | Expected proof |
| --- | --- |
| Unit | TypeScript build |
| Integration | Curl CRUD smoke checks against running Express server |
| E2E | Not required for API-only slice |
| Platform | Not required |
| Release | Not required |

## Harness Delta

None.

## Evidence

- `npm run build` passed.
- HTTP smoke checks against `npm run dev` returned: health `200`, create
  validation `400`, create `201`, list `200`, get `200`, update `200`,
  missing get/update/delete `404`, delete `204`.
- SQLite check confirmed the `bookmarks` table exists in `data.db`.

./benchmark/runs/phase-3-active-observability-cli-1.4.1-rerun-20260531/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:12:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_6","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' docs/HARNESS.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Harness\n\nThe project goal is to provide a reusable operating harness that lets humans and\nagents turn a future product spec into safe, validated work.\n\nThe app is what users touch. The harness is what agents touch.\n\n## Mental Model\n\n```text\n------------------+\n| Human intent    |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Feature intake   |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Story packet     |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Agent work loop  |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Product delta    |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Validation proof |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Harness delta    |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Next intent      |\n+------------------+\n```\n\nEvery task has two possible outputs:\n\n1. Product delta: app code, tests, API shape, data model, or product docs.\n2. Harness delta: docs, templates, validation expectations, backlog items, or\n   decision records that make the next task easier.\n\n## Harness v0 Scope\n\nHarness v0 includes:\n\n- Agent entrypoint.\n- Empty product documentation structure.\n- Feature intake and risk lanes.\n- Story templates.\n- Decision log template.\n- Validation report template.\n- Test matrix placeholder.\n- Harness growth backlog.\n- Durable layer: SQLite database and CLI for operational records.\n\nHarness v0 deliberately excludes:\n\n- A project-specific `SPEC.md`.\n- Pre-sliced product domains.\n- A locked application stack.\n- App source scaffolding.\n- Package scripts.\n- Test runner config.\n- CI workflows.\n\nThose should arrive only when a selected story needs them.\n\n## Durable Layer\n\nPolicy documents describe how to work. The durable layer stores what happened.\n\nOperational data — intake classifications, story status, decision outcomes,\nbacklog items, and execution traces — lives in a SQLite database (`harness.db`)\nmanaged by the Rust Harness CLI at `scripts/bin/harness-cli`. Agents and humans\nshould use that binary for Harness work. The database is local to each project\ninstance and `.gitignore`d. The schema is version-controlled under\n`scripts/schema/`.\n\nThis separation keeps policy docs stable and human-readable while giving agents\na structured, queryable record of operational state. It also prepares the\nharness for future observability and automated evolution without adding more\nmarkdown files.\n\nInitialize the database if it does not exist:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli init\n```\n\nCommon commands:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli intake  --type <type> --summary <text> --lane <lane>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli story   add --id <id> --title <text> --lane <lane>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli story   update --id <id> --status <status>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli trace   --summary <text> --outcome <outcome>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli score-trace\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query   matrix\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query   backlog\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query   stats\n```\n\n## Source Hierarchy\n\n```text\nUser-provided spec or prompt\n  input material for first buildout or future changes\n\ndocs/product/*\n  current product contract derived from accepted input\n\ndocs/stories/*\n  story-sized work packets and historical evidence\n\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query matrix\n  behavior-to-proof control panel backed by the durable layer\n\ndocs/decisions/*\n  why the contract changed\n```\n\nBefore implementation, product docs describe intent. After implementation,\nproduct docs plus executable tests become the living contract.\n\n## Spec Lifecycle\n\nHarness v0 starts without a tracked project spec. When the human provides a\nspecification, treat it as input material, not as a permanent operating manual.\nUse it to populate product docs, story packets, architecture decisions, and\nvalidation expectations during the first buildout.\n\nAfter the specification has been decomposed, do not keep extending it as the\nliving product plan. Ongoing work should update the smaller product docs,\nstories, durable proof records, and decision records.\n\nOngoing work should enter the harness as one of these input types:\n\n- New spec: a project specification that needs to become product docs and\n  initial story candidates.\n- Spec slice: a selected behavior from the provided spec.\n- Change request: a bounded behavior change, bug fix, or product refinement.\n- New initiative: a larger product area that needs multiple stories.\n- Maintenance request: dependency, architecture, performance, security, or\n  operational work.\n- Harness improvement: a process, template, proof, or agent-instruction change.\n\nThe spec-to-work loop is:\n\n```text\nhuman intent or supplied spec\n  -> classify input type\n  -> update or create product contract\n  -> create story packet or initiative notes when needed\n  -> define validation proof\n  -> implement or document the blocker\n  -> update product docs, stories, durable proof records, and decisions\n  -> capture harness friction\n```\n\nLarge product areas should use scoped initiative notes instead of a second\nmonolithic specification. An initiative should explain the goal, affected\nproduct docs, candidate stories, validation shape, open decisions, and exit\ncriteria. If initiative work becomes a repeated pattern, add a template or\nrecord the proposal with `scripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add`.\n\n## Growth Rule\n\nThe harness grows from friction.\n\nWhen an agent is confused, repeats manual reasoning, needs a new validation\ncommand, discovers a missing rule, or sees a recurring failure pattern, it must\neither improve the harness directly or record the friction:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add --title \"<short name>\" --pain \"<what was hard>\"\n```\n\nUse the backlog outcome loop for improvements that are expected to change agent\nbehavior or validation results:\n\n1. When creating the backlog item, fill `--predicted` with the measurable\n   impact expected from the improvement.\n2. When closing the item, fill `--outcome` with the actual measured result or\n   review evidence.\n3. Use `scripts/harness query backlog --open` to review proposed and accepted\n   items, and `scripts/harness query backlog --closed` to compare predictions\n   with outcomes after implementation.\n\nThe `harness_friction` field on traces also captures per-task friction so\npatterns can be queried later:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query friction\n```\n\n## Task Loop\n\nFor every task:\n\n1. Classify the request with `docs/FEATURE_INTAKE.md`.\n2. Record the classification with `scripts/bin/harness-cli intake`.\n3. Locate the affected product docs and story files.\n4. Check proof status with `scripts/bin/harness-cli query matrix`.\n5. Work only inside the selected lane: tiny, normal, or high-risk.\n6. Before finishing, ask whether product truth, validation expectations,\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
./benchmark/runs/phase-3-active-observability-cli-1.4.1-rerun-20260531/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:14:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_4","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' docs/CONTEXT_RULES.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Context Engineering Rules\n\nContext rules help agents decide what to read, when to read it, and when to\nstop reading. They are additive to the stable `AGENTS.md` reading list.\n\nThe goal is not to maximize context. The goal is to put the right information\nin the model for the current task phase and risk lane.\n\n## Context Phases\n\n### Intake Phase\n\nRead to classify the request, find the affected surface, and choose a lane.\n\n| Document Or Source | Tiny | Normal | High-Risk |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| `AGENTS.md` | Must | Must | Must |\n| `docs/FEATURE_INTAKE.md` | Must | Must | Must |\n| `scripts/harness query matrix` | Must | Must | Must |\n| `README.md` | Should | Must | Must |\n| `docs/HARNESS.md` | Should | Must | Must |\n| `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` | Skip | Should | Must |\n| Relevant `docs/product/*` | Skip if unrelated | Must if product behavior changes | Must |\n| Relevant `docs/stories/*` | Skip if unrelated | Must if a story exists | Must |\n| `docs/decisions/*` | Skip | Should if architecture or durable rules are touched | Must |\n| `docs/HARNESS_COMPONENTS.md` | Skip | Should for Harness improvements | Must for observability or benchmark work |\n\n### Planning Phase\n\nRead to decide the smallest safe approach and expected proof.\n\n| Document Or Source | Tiny | Normal | High-Risk |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| Current files to edit | Must | Must | Must |\n| `docs/templates/story.md` | Skip | Must when creating/updating a story | Should |\n| `docs/templates/high-risk-story/*` | Skip | Skip unless risk escalates | Must |\n| `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` | Skip | Should for code or boundary changes | Must |\n| `docs/TEST_MATRIX.md` or `scripts/harness query matrix` | Should | Must | Must |\n| Relevant decisions | Skip | Should | Must |\n| `docs/HARNESS_MATURITY.md` | Skip | Should for Harness improvements | Must for maturity or process changes |\n| `docs/HARNESS_BACKLOG.md` and `scripts/harness query backlog` | Skip | Should if friction repeats | Must if changing Harness behavior |\n\n### Implementation Phase\n\nRead while making the change. Keep this phase scoped to files that directly\naffect the selected story.\n\n| Document Or Source | Tiny | Normal | High-Risk |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| Files being changed | Must | Must | Must |\n| Adjacent files with same pattern | Should | Must | Must |\n| Relevant product docs | Skip if copy-only | Must if behavior changes | Must |\n| Relevant story packet | Skip if no story needed | Must | Must |\n| Relevant templates | Skip | Should when adding docs | Must |\n| `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` | Skip | Should for structural changes | Must |\n| Provider/API/security docs | Skip | Should if touched | Must |\n| Unrelated docs and historical traces | Skip | Skip | Should only if they affect decisions |\n\n### Validation Phase\n\nRead to prove the change and avoid claiming unsupported completion.\n\n| Document Or Source | Tiny | Normal | High-Risk |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| Story acceptance criteria | Should | Must | Must |\n| `docs/TEST_MATRIX.md` or `scripts/harness query matrix` | Should | Must | Must |\n| Validation section of story packet | Skip if no story | Must | Must |\n| `docs/templates/validation-report.md` | Skip | Should for notable proof | Must for high-risk proof |\n| Relevant commands from README/package docs | Should | Must | Must |\n| Benchmark protocol or external benchmark repo | Skip | Skip unless requested | Must if the story depends on benchmark proof |\n| `docs/HARNESS_MATURITY.md` | Skip | Should for Harness improvements | Must for maturity claims |\n\n### Trace Phase\n\nRead to leave useful evidence for the next agent and for benchmark scoring.\n\n| Document Or Source | Tiny | Normal | High-Risk |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| `docs/TRACE_SPEC.md` | Should | Must | Must |\n| `scripts/harness query matrix` | Should | Must | Must |\n| `scripts/harness query backlog` | Skip | Should if friction occurred | Must |\n| Changed-file list from `git status --short` | Must | Must | Must |\n| Validation command output | Should | Must | Must |\n| Story packet or progress log | Skip if no story | Must | Must |\n| `docs/HARNESS_COMPONENTS.md` | Skip | Should if attributing friction | Must if failure attribution is needed |\n\n## Retrieval Triggers\n\n| Trigger Condition | Action |\n| --- | --- |\n| Task touches database schema, durable records, or migrations | Read `docs/decisions/0004-sqlite-durable-layer.md`, `scripts/schema/`, and relevant CLI code before planning. |\n| Task touches CLI command behavior or installer distribution | Read `docs/decisions/0005-prebuilt-rust-harness-cli.md`, `scripts/README.md`, `scripts/harness`, and installer docs. |\n| Task touches auth, authorization, audit/security, data loss, or external providers | Treat as high-risk, read `docs/templates/high-risk-story/*`, and check prior decisions before implementation. |\n| Task changes public API shape, product behavior, or user-visible workflow | Read relevant `docs/product/*`, story packets, and validation expectations before editing. |\n| Task changes Harness policy, source hierarchy, risk classification, or validation requirements | Read `docs/HARNESS.md`, `docs/FEATURE_INTAKE.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, and `docs/decisions/*`; pause if direction is ambiguous. |\n| Task discovers repeated confusion, stale docs, or missing proof | Read `docs/HARNESS_BACKLOG.md`, record `harness_friction`, and add a backlog item when the fix is out of scope. |\n| Task makes a maturity, observability, trace quality, or benchmark claim | Read `docs/HARNESS_COMPONENTS.md`, `docs/HARNESS_MATURITY.md`, and `docs/TRACE_SPEC.md`. |\n| Task is normal or high-risk and spans multiple iterations | Create or update a story/progress file under `docs/stories/` and keep it current. |\n| Final response is being prepared | Re-read the validation evidence, `git status --short`, and `docs/TRACE_SPEC.md` before recording the final trace. |\n\n## Token Budget Guidance\n\n| Lane | Target Context Budget | Read Shape | Reasoning |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| Tiny | About 2K tokens of Harness context | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/FEATURE_INTAKE.md`, matrix query, and the exact file being changed. | Tiny work should not spend more context on policy than on the edit. |\n| Normal | About 5K tokens of Harness context | Intake docs, relevant product/story docs, architecture when structural, validation expectations, and trace spec at the end. | Normal work needs enough context to preserve contracts and record proof without reading every historical file. |\n| High-risk | About 10K tokens of Harness context | Full intake, architecture, relevant decisions, high-risk templates, product docs, validation docs, trace spec, and component/maturity docs when Harness behavior changes. | High-risk work needs source hierarchy, prior decisions, and proof expectations in context before implementation. |\n\nBudget rules:\n\n- Prefer targeted `rg` searches over bulk reading.\n- Read the smallest section that answers the current phase question.\n- Escalate context when a retrieval trigger fires.\n- Do not keep reading unrelated history after the lane, affected files, and\n  validation path are clear.\n\n## Additive Behavior\n\nThese rules do not replace `AGENTS.md`. Agents should still read the stable\nentrypoint documents listed there before work. This document explains what to\nretrieve after that initial context, based on lane, phase, and trigger.\n\n## Review Checklist\n\nBefore implementation:\n\n- Lane is chosen from `docs/FEATURE_INTAKE.md`.\n- Relevant product docs or story packets are identified.\n- Any high-risk trigger has been handled.\n\nBefore final response:\n\n- Validation evidence has been read.\n- `docs/TRACE_SPEC.md` has been read for normal/high-risk tasks.\n- The final trace includes files read, files changed, outcome, and friction\n  when applicable.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
./benchmark/runs/phase-3-active-observability-cli-1.4.1-rerun-20260531/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:16:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_7","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' docs/ARCHITECTURE.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Architecture\n\nNo application stack is selected yet.\n\nNo application code exists yet. This document defines generic architecture\nquestions and boundary rules that future implementation should adapt after a\nuser-provided spec and stack decision exist.\n\n## Discovery Before Shape\n\nBefore proposing implementation shape, identify:\n\n- Product surfaces: browser, mobile, desktop, CLI, API, worker, or service.\n- Runtime stack: language, framework, database, queues, providers, and hosting.\n- Core domains: the product concepts that deserve stable names and contracts.\n- Boundary inputs: user input, API requests, webhooks, jobs, files, credentials,\n  provider payloads, and environment configuration.\n- Validation ladder: the smallest checks that can prove the selected stack.\n\nRecord stack choices in `docs/decisions/` when they meaningfully constrain\nfuture work.\n\n## Default Layering\n\n```text\ndomain\n  <- application\n      <- infrastructure\n          <- interface\n              <- app surfaces\n```\n\n## Candidate Structure\n\n```text\napp/\n  domain/\n    entities/\n    value-objects/\n    repositories/\n    services/\n\n  application/\n    commands/\n    queries/\n    handlers/\n\n  infrastructure/\n    database/\n    logging/\n    notifications/\n\n  interface/\n    controllers/\n    dto/\n    presenters/\n    routes/\n    middlewares/\n\nsurfaces/\n  browser/\n  mobile/\n  desktop/\n  cli/\n```\n\nThis is a thinking template, not a scaffold. Create real folders only when a\nstory enters implementation and the selected stack needs them.\n\n## Dependency Rule\n\nInner layers must not depend on outer layers.\n\n| Layer | May depend on | Must not depend on |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| domain | nothing project-external except tiny pure utilities | framework, database, UI, provider, process/env |\n| application | domain | framework, UI, provider, database concrete clients |\n| infrastructure | domain, application | interface controllers or UI |\n| interface | all backend layers | UI state or platform shell assumptions |\n| app surfaces | API contracts and app-facing clients | domain internals directly |\n\n## Parse-First Boundary Rule\n\nUnknown data must be parsed at boundaries before it enters inner code.\n\nBoundaries include:\n\n- HTTP request bodies, params, and query strings.\n- Session payloads and identity claims.\n- Environment variables.\n- Database rows returned from external clients.\n- Platform shell payloads.\n- Deep links, tokens, and signed URLs.\n- Provider webhooks, events, and async payloads.\n\nTarget flow:\n\n```text\nunknown input\n  -> parser\n  -> typed DTO or command\n  -> application use case\n  -> domain object/value object\n```\n\nInner layers should work with meaningful product types such as `UserId`,\n`AccountId`, `WorkspaceId`, `Role`, `DateRange`, or domain-specific IDs,\nrather than repeatedly validating raw strings.\n\n## Command/Query Boundary\n\nIf the product has both reads and writes, keep command/query separation clear at\nthe code level even when the storage layer is simple:\n\n- Commands mutate state and own audit side effects.\n- Queries read state and format for consumers.\n- Shared domain rules live in domain/application, not controllers.\n\n## Observability Contract\n\nThe future server should emit one canonical JSON log line per request with:\n\n- timestamp\n- level\n- request_id\n- user_id when known\n- action\n- duration_ms\n- status_code\n- message\n\nAudit logs are product records. Application logs are operational records. Do not\nuse one as a substitute for the other.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
./benchmark/runs/phase-3-active-observability-cli-1.4.1-rerun-20260531/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:20:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_9","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API\n\n## Overview\n\nA REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.\n\n## Functional Requirements\n\n### Core Entities\n\n- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps\n- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks\n- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders\n\n### API Endpoints\n\n#### Health\n- `GET /health` → `{ \"status\": \"ok\" }`\n\n#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)\n- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)\n- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)\n- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark\n- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark\n- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark\n\n#### Folders\n- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)\n- `GET /folders` — List all folders\n- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks\n- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name\n- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)\n\n#### Authentication\n- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)\n- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token\n- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented\n\n### Validation Rules\n\n- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL\n- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string\n- Folder `name` must be non-empty string\n- Email must be valid format\n- Password must be >= 8 characters\n\n### Pagination (after T6)\n\n- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`\n- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`\n- Default: page=1, limit=20\n- Max limit: 100\n\n## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)\n\n- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+\n- **Language**: TypeScript\n- **Framework**: Express\n- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3\n- **Testing**: Vitest\n- **Port**: 3000\n\n## Non-Functional Requirements\n\n- All responses are JSON\n- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)\n- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)\n- JWT tokens for session management\n- Database file: `data.db` (application data)\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

./benchmark/runs/phase-3-active-observability-cli-1.4.1-rerun-20260531/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:31:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_15","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' docs/product/bookmarks.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Bookmarks\n\nBookmarks are saved URLs with a title, optional description, optional folder, and creation/update timestamps. Folders are named collections of bookmarks. Users own their bookmarks and folders.\n\n## API Contract\n\n- `POST /auth/register` creates a user from valid `email` and `password`, stores a password hash, and returns the user without `password_hash`.\n- `POST /auth/login` authenticates a user from valid `email` and `password` and returns a JWT token.\n- All `/bookmarks` and `/folders` endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return `401` when the token is missing or invalid.\n- `POST /bookmarks` creates an authenticated user's bookmark from `url`, `title`, optional `description`, and optional `folder_id`.\n- `GET /bookmarks` returns the authenticated user's bookmarks as a JSON array.\n- `GET /bookmarks/:id` returns one owned bookmark or `404` when it does not exist for that user.\n- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` updates an owned bookmark, including optional `folder_id`, and returns it, or `404` when it does not exist for that user.\n- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` removes an owned bookmark and returns `204`, or `404` when it does not exist for that user.\n- `POST /folders` creates an authenticated user's folder from required `name` and returns `201`.\n- `GET /folders` returns the authenticated user's folders as a JSON array.\n- `GET /folders/:id` returns one owned folder with a `bookmarks` array or `404` when it does not exist for that user.\n- `PUT /folders/:id` updates an owned folder name and returns it, or `404` when it does not exist for that user.\n- `DELETE /folders/:id` removes an owned folder and returns `204`, or `404` when it does not exist for that user. Owned bookmarks in the deleted folder are not deleted; their `folder_id` becomes `null`.\n\n## Authentication\n\n- Register and login require a valid email address and a password of at least 8 characters.\n- Duplicate registration returns `409`.\n- Invalid login credentials return `401`.\n- JWT payloads include `userId`.\n- Authenticated bookmark and folder responses do not include password hashes.\n\n## Data Contract\n\nBookmark records are stored in SQLite `data.db` in the `bookmarks` table:\n\n- `id`: integer primary key\n- `user_id`: required integer foreign key to `users.id`\n- `url`: required text\n- `title`: required text\n- `description`: nullable text\n- `folder_id`: nullable integer foreign key to `folders.id`\n- `created_at`: ISO timestamp text\n- `updated_at`: ISO timestamp text\n\nFolder records are stored in SQLite `data.db` in the `folders` table:\n\n- `id`: integer primary key\n- `user_id`: required integer foreign key to `users.id`\n- `name`: required text\n- `created_at`: ISO timestamp text\n- `updated_at`: ISO timestamp text\n\nUser records are stored in SQLite `data.db` in the `users` table:\n\n- `id`: integer primary key\n- `email`: required unique text\n- `password_hash`: required text\n- `created_at`: ISO timestamp text\n\n## Validation\n\n- `url` is required for creation and must be a valid URL.\n- `title` is required for creation and must be a non-empty string.\n- Empty titles are rejected with `400`.\n- `folder_id`, when provided, must reference an existing folder or the request returns `400`.\n- `folder_id`, when provided, must reference a folder owned by the authenticated user.\n- Folder `name` is required and must be a non-empty string.\n- `email` is required for registration and login and must be a valid email address.\n- `password` is required for registration and login and must be at least 8 characters.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

`description` is nullable.\n- UI surfaces: none.\n\n## Validation\n\n| Layer | Expected proof |\n| --- | --- |\n| Unit | TypeScript build catches route/database typing issues. |\n| Integration | Curl checks against local server cover create, list, read, update, delete, validation, health, and SQLite persistence. |\n| E2E | Not applicable; API-only task. |\n| Platform | Not applicable beyond local Node/SQLite runtime. |\n| Release | Not applicable. |\n\n## Harness Delta\n\nAdded the story packet and bookmark product contract for the T2 slice.\n\n## Evidence\n\n- `npm run build`\n- Local HTTP checks on `PORT=3100`: health `200`, create `201`, list `200`, get `200`, update `200`, delete `204`, deleted get `404`.\n- Validation checks on `POST /bookmarks`: missing title `400`, missing url `400`, empty title `400`.\n- Direct SQLite check confirmed the `bookmarks` schema and a persisted row in `data.db`.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Bookmarks may optionally reference a folder, and deleting a folder leaves its bookmarks in place with `folder_id` set to `null`.\n\n## Relevant Product Docs\n\n- `PRODUCT_SPEC.md`\n- `docs/product/bookmarks.md`\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n\n- `POST /folders` creates a folder from required `name` and returns `201`.\n- `GET /folders` returns `200` with an array of folders.\n- `GET /folders/:id` returns `200` with a folder plus its `bookmarks` array, or `404`.\n- `PUT /folders/:id` updates folder `name` and returns `200`, or `404`.\n- `DELETE /folders/:id` returns `204`, or `404`, and nullifies matching bookmark `folder_id` values.\n- `POST /bookmarks` and `PUT /bookmarks/:id` accept optional `folder_id`.\n- Non-existent bookmark `folder_id` values return `400`.\n- Existing bookmark endpoints continue to work.\n\n## Design Notes\n\n- Commands: create, update, delete folders; create/update bookmarks with optional folder reference.\n- Queries: list folders, fetch folder by id, fetch bookmarks for folder, validate folder existence.\n- API: Express JSON handlers under `/folders` plus updated `/bookmarks` handlers.\n- Tables: SQLite `folders` table; nullable `bookmarks.folder_id` foreign key.\n- Domain rules: folder `name` must be a non-empty string; `folder_id` must be `null`, omitted, or an existing positive integer folder id.\n- UI surfaces: none.\n\n## Validation\n\n| Layer | Expected proof |\n| --- | --- |\n| Unit | TypeScript build catches route/database typing issues. |\n| Integration | Local HTTP checks cover folder CRUD, bookmark folder assignment, invalid folder validation, delete nullification, and bookmark regression. |\n| E2E | Not applicable; API-only task. |\n| Platform | Not applicable beyond local Node/SQLite runtime. |\n| Release | Not applicable. |\n\n## Harness Delta\n\nAdded a story packet for the T3 folder support slice.\n\n## Evidence\n\n- `npm run build`\n- Manual local HTTP checks on `PORT=3100`: create folder `201`, create bookmark with `folder_id` `201`, get folder with one bookmark `200`, update folder `200`, list folders array `200`, invalid `folder_id` `400`, bookmark list regression `200`, delete folder `204`, deleted folder bookmark `folder_id` became `null`, bookmark validation regressions `400`, health `200`.\n- `benchmark/lib/check-functional.sh` T3 check: create folder, list folders, get folder, create bookmark with folder, and bookmark list regression all passed.\n- `npm test` currently exits with code 1 because no Vitest test files exist.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

./benchmark/runs/phase-3-active-observability-cli-1.4.1-rerun-20260531/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:70:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_38","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' docs/TRACE_SPEC.md 2>/dev/null || true\"","aggregated_output":"# Trace Specification\n\nThe `trace` table records what happened during a Harness task. This document\ndefines the expected depth and format for each field so traces are useful for\nreview, benchmark scoring, failure attribution, and future harness evolution.\n\nThe current schema lives in `scripts/schema/001-init.sql` under the `trace`\ntable. The schema is not changed by Phase 2.\n\n## Field Reference\n\n| Field | Type | Required | Format | Example |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| `id` | INTEGER | Automatic | SQLite autoincrement primary key. Do not set manually. | `42` |\n| `created_at` | TEXT | Automatic | SQLite `datetime('now')`. Do not set manually. | `2026-05-27 09:24:37` |\n| `task_summary` | TEXT | Yes | One sentence, at least 10 characters, naming the outcome or attempted outcome. | `Completed Phase 2 docs-only observability and taxonomy specification` |\n| `intake_id` | INTEGER | Standard+ when an intake was recorded | Integer id from the related `intake` row. | `36` |\n| `story_id` | TEXT | Standard+ when work maps to one story | Story id from the `story` table. Use the main story when one trace covers several; list the rest in `notes`. | `US-004` |\n| `agent` | TEXT | Optional for minimal; Standard+ expected | Short agent/tool name. | `codex` |\n| `actions_taken` | TEXT | Standard+ | JSON array text. With the current CLI, pass a comma-separated list and the CLI stores JSON text. | `[\"read PHASE2.md\",\"drafted TRACE_SPEC.md\",\"updated HARNESS.md\"]` |\n| `files_read` | TEXT | Standard+ | JSON array text of paths or command names. With the current CLI, pass a comma-separated list. | `[\"PHASE2.md\",\"docs/HARNESS.md\",\"scripts/harness query matrix\"]` |\n| `files_changed` | TEXT | Standard+ | JSON array text of changed file paths. With the current CLI, pass a comma-separated list; omit only when no files changed. | `[\"docs/TRACE_SPEC.md\",\"docs/HARNESS.md\"]` |\n| `decisions_made` | TEXT | Detailed | JSON array text of decision strings. Include scope decisions, validation choices, and explicit non-goals. | `[\"Kept Phase 2 docs-only; installer propagation remains out of scope\"]` |\n| `errors` | TEXT | Standard+ if errors occurred; Detailed always | JSON array text of error or blocker strings. Until the CLI supports empty arrays directly, use `none` when a detailed trace needs explicit no-error evidence. | `[\"git diff --check failed before whitespace fix\"]` |\n| `outcome` | TEXT | Yes before final response | One of `completed`, `blocked`, `partial`, or `failed`. | `completed` |\n| `duration_seconds` | INTEGER | Detailed when available | Positive integer estimate or measured duration. Leave null if unknown. | `1800` |\n| `token_estimate` | INTEGER | Detailed when available | Positive integer estimate. Leave null if unknown. | `24000` |\n| `harness_friction` | TEXT | Standard+ when friction exists; Detailed always | Free text naming what was hard, missing, ambiguous, or repeated. Use `none` only when the agent actively checked and found no friction. | `New Phase 2 docs are not in installer copy list; recorded as out-of-scope follow-up.` |\n| `notes` | TEXT | Optional | Free text for review context that does not fit other fields. | `Trace covers US-003, US-004, US-005, and US-006.` |\n\n## Quality Tiers\n\n### Minimal (score: 1)\n\nMinimum fields:\n\n- `task_summary` is filled and at least 10 characters.\n- `outcome` is filled before the final response.\n\nAcceptable for:\n\n- Tiny-lane tasks with no file changes or only low-risk copy/doc edits.\n\nNot acceptable for:\n\n- Normal or high-risk work.\n- Any work that discovered friction, errors, or a missing validation path.\n\n### Standard (score: 2)\n\nMinimum fields:\n\n- All Minimal fields.\n- `intake_id` when an intake was recorded.\n- `story_id` when the work maps cleanly to one story.\n- `agent`.\n- `actions_taken` as JSON array text.\n- `files_read` as JSON array text.\n- `files_changed` as JSON array text.\n- At least one of `errors` or `harness_friction`.\n\nRequired for:\n\n- Normal-lane tasks.\n- Tiny tasks that changed Harness instructions, validation expectations, or\n  durable records.\n\nStandard traces may leave `duration_seconds`, `token_estimate`, and\n`decisions_made` empty when those details are not useful.\n\n### Detailed (score: 3)\n\nMinimum fields:\n\n- All Standard fields.\n- `decisions_made` as JSON array text.\n- `errors` as JSON array text, using `none` with the current CLI when no\n  errors occurred.\n- `harness_friction`, using `none` only after checking for friction.\n- `duration_seconds` or a note explaining why duration is unavailable.\n- `token_estimate` or a note explaining why token estimate is unavailable.\n- `notes` when one trace covers multiple stories, multiple risk flags, or\n  skipped validation.\n\nRequired for:\n\n- High-risk tasks.\n- Changes touching architecture direction, source-of-truth hierarchy,\n  validation requirements, auth, authorization, data loss, audit/security, or\n  external provider behavior.\n- Benchmark or release work where later review needs precise proof.\n\n## Lane Mapping\n\n| Lane | Expected Tier | Minimum Trace Behavior |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| Tiny | Minimal | Record summary and outcome; use Standard if friction or Harness docs changed. |\n| Normal | Standard | Record intake, actions, files read, files changed, outcome, and friction/errors. |\n| High-risk | Detailed | Record all fields or explicitly explain unavailable duration/token estimates. |\n\n## Friction Capture Protocol\n\nPopulate `harness_friction` when any of these occur:\n\n- The agent had to infer a missing rule or source of truth.\n- Required validation was unclear, unavailable, or too expensive to run.\n- A document, durable record, or story packet was stale or contradictory.\n- The task revealed a repeated manual step that should become a template,\n  command, or checklist.\n- A requested change was out of scope but likely important later.\n- A benchmark or review failure could not be attributed to a component.\n\nHow to write friction:\n\n- Name the concrete pain, not a vague mood.\n- Include the missing capability or contradiction.\n- If the friction should become work, also add or update a backlog item with\n  `scripts/harness backlog add`.\n- If there was no friction, use `none` only for Detailed traces.\n\nGood friction:\n\n```text\nNew Phase 2 docs are not copied by scripts/install-harness.sh, but installer\npropagation is out of scope for docs-only Phase 2.\n```\n\nWeak friction:\n\n```text\ndocs confusing\n```\n\n## Examples\n\n### Good Trace (Detailed)\n\n```bash\nscripts/harness trace \\\n  --summary \"Completed high-risk auth role migration with audit proof\" \\\n  --intake 51 \\\n  --story US-014 \\\n  --agent codex \\\n  --outcome completed \\\n  --duration 4200 \\\n  --tokens 52000 \\\n  --actions \"read access-control docs,created migration,updated
