./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260527/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:15:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_6","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,280p' docs/ARCHITECTURE.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Architecture\n\nNo application stack is selected yet.\n\nNo application code exists yet. This document defines generic architecture\nquestions and boundary rules that future implementation should adapt after a\nuser-provided spec and stack decision exist.\n\n## Discovery Before Shape\n\nBefore proposing implementation shape, identify:\n\n- Product surfaces: browser, mobile, desktop, CLI, API, worker, or service.\n- Runtime stack: language, framework, database, queues, providers, and hosting.\n- Core domains: the product concepts that deserve stable names and contracts.\n- Boundary inputs: user input, API requests, webhooks, jobs, files, credentials,\n  provider payloads, and environment configuration.\n- Validation ladder: the smallest checks that can prove the selected stack.\n\nRecord stack choices in `docs/decisions/` when they meaningfully constrain\nfuture work.\n\n## Default Layering\n\n```text\ndomain\n  <- application\n      <- infrastructure\n          <- interface\n              <- app surfaces\n```\n\n## Candidate Structure\n\n```text\napp/\n  domain/\n    entities/\n    value-objects/\n    repositories/\n    services/\n\n  application/\n    commands/\n    queries/\n    handlers/\n\n  infrastructure/\n    database/\n    logging/\n    notifications/\n\n  interface/\n    controllers/\n    dto/\n    presenters/\n    routes/\n    middlewares/\n\nsurfaces/\n  browser/\n  mobile/\n  desktop/\n  cli/\n```\n\nThis is a thinking template, not a scaffold. Create real folders only when a\nstory enters implementation and the selected stack needs them.\n\n## Dependency Rule\n\nInner layers must not depend on outer layers.\n\n| Layer | May depend on | Must not depend on |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| domain | nothing project-external except tiny pure utilities | framework, database, UI, provider, process/env |\n| application | domain | framework, UI, provider, database concrete clients |\n| infrastructure | domain, application | interface controllers or UI |\n| interface | all backend layers | UI state or platform shell assumptions |\n| app surfaces | API contracts and app-facing clients | domain internals directly |\n\n## Parse-First Boundary Rule\n\nUnknown data must be parsed at boundaries before it enters inner code.\n\nBoundaries include:\n\n- HTTP request bodies, params, and query strings.\n- Session payloads and identity claims.\n- Environment variables.\n- Database rows returned from external clients.\n- Platform shell payloads.\n- Deep links, tokens, and signed URLs.\n- Provider webhooks, events, and async payloads.\n\nTarget flow:\n\n```text\nunknown input\n  -> parser\n  -> typed DTO or command\n  -> application use case\n  -> domain object/value object\n```\n\nInner layers should work with meaningful product types such as `UserId`,\n`AccountId`, `WorkspaceId`, `Role`, `DateRange`, or domain-specific IDs,\nrather than repeatedly validating raw strings.\n\n## Command/Query Boundary\n\nIf the product has both reads and writes, keep command/query separation clear at\nthe code level even when the storage layer is simple:\n\n- Commands mutate state and own audit side effects.\n- Queries read state and format for consumers.\n- Shared domain rules live in domain/application, not controllers.\n\n## Observability Contract\n\nThe future server should emit one canonical JSON log line per request with:\n\n- timestamp\n- level\n- request_id\n- user_id when known\n- action\n- duration_ms\n- status_code\n- message\n\nAudit logs are product records. Application logs are operational records. Do not\nuse one as a substitute for the other.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260527/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:16:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_8","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' docs/HARNESS.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Harness\n\nThe project goal is to provide a reusable operating harness that lets humans and\nagents turn a future product spec into safe, validated work.\n\nThe app is what users touch. The harness is what agents touch.\n\n## Mental Model\n\n```text\n------------------+\n| Human intent    |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Feature intake   |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Story packet     |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Agent work loop  |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Product delta    |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Validation proof |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Harness delta    |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Next intent      |\n+------------------+\n```\n\nEvery task has two possible outputs:\n\n1. Product delta: app code, tests, API shape, data model, or product docs.\n2. Harness delta: docs, templates, validation expectations, backlog items, or\n   decision records that make the next task easier.\n\n## Harness v0 Scope\n\nHarness v0 includes:\n\n- Agent entrypoint.\n- Empty product documentation structure.\n- Feature intake and risk lanes.\n- Story templates.\n- Decision log template.\n- Validation report template.\n- Test matrix placeholder.\n- Harness growth backlog.\n- Durable layer: SQLite database and CLI for operational records.\n\nHarness v0 deliberately excludes:\n\n- A project-specific `SPEC.md`.\n- Pre-sliced product domains.\n- A locked application stack.\n- App source scaffolding.\n- Package scripts.\n- Test runner config.\n- CI workflows.\n\nThose should arrive only when a selected story needs them.\n\n## Durable Layer\n\nPolicy documents describe how to work. The durable layer stores what happened.\n\nOperational data — intake classifications, story status, decision outcomes,\nbacklog items, and execution traces — lives in a SQLite database (`harness.db`)\nmanaged by the Rust Harness CLI at `scripts/bin/harness-cli`. Agents and humans\nshould use that binary for Harness work. The database is local to each project\ninstance and `.gitignore`d. The schema is version-controlled under\n`scripts/schema/`.\n\nThis separation keeps policy docs stable and human-readable while giving agents\na structured, queryable record of operational state. It also prepares the\nharness for future observability and automated evolution without adding more\nmarkdown files.\n\nInitialize the database if it does not exist:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli init\n```\n\nCommon commands:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli intake  --type <type> --summary <text> --lane <lane>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli story   add --id <id> --title <text> --lane <lane>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli story   update --id <id> --status <status>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli trace   --summary <text> --outcome <outcome>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query   matrix\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query   backlog\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query   stats\n```\n\n## Source Hierarchy\n\n```text\nUser-provided spec or prompt\n  input material for first buildout or future changes\n\ndocs/product/*\n  current product contract derived from accepted input\n\ndocs/stories/*\n  story-sized work packets and historical evidence\n\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query matrix\n  behavior-to-proof control panel backed by the durable layer\n\ndocs/decisions/*\n  why the contract changed\n```\n\nBefore implementation, product docs describe intent. After implementation,\nproduct docs plus executable tests become the living contract.\n\n## Spec Lifecycle\n\nHarness v0 starts without a tracked project spec. When the human provides a\nspecification, treat it as input material, not as a permanent operating manual.\nUse it to populate product docs, story packets, architecture decisions, and\nvalidation expectations during the first buildout.\n\nAfter the specification has been decomposed, do not keep extending it as the\nliving product plan. Ongoing work should update the smaller product docs,\nstories, durable proof records, and decision records.\n\nOngoing work should enter the harness as one of these input types:\n\n- New spec: a project specification that needs to become product docs and\n  initial story candidates.\n- Spec slice: a selected behavior from the provided spec.\n- Change request: a bounded behavior change, bug fix, or product refinement.\n- New initiative: a larger product area that needs multiple stories.\n- Maintenance request: dependency, architecture, performance, security, or\n  operational work.\n- Harness improvement: a process, template, proof, or agent-instruction change.\n\nThe spec-to-work loop is:\n\n```text\nhuman intent or supplied spec\n  -> classify input type\n  -> update or create product contract\n  -> create story packet or initiative notes when needed\n  -> define validation proof\n  -> implement or document the blocker\n  -> update product docs, stories, durable proof records, and decisions\n  -> capture harness friction\n```\n\nLarge product areas should use scoped initiative notes instead of a second\nmonolithic specification. An initiative should explain the goal, affected\nproduct docs, candidate stories, validation shape, open decisions, and exit\ncriteria. If initiative work becomes a repeated pattern, add a template or\nrecord the proposal with `scripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add`.\n\n## Growth Rule\n\nThe harness grows from friction.\n\nWhen an agent is confused, repeats manual reasoning, needs a new validation\ncommand, discovers a missing rule, or sees a recurring failure pattern, it must\neither improve the harness directly or record the friction:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add --title \"<short name>\" --pain \"<what was hard>\"\n```\n\nThe `harness_friction` field on traces also captures per-task friction so\npatterns can be queried later:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query friction\n```\n\n## Task Loop\n\nFor every task:\n\n1. Classify the request with `docs/FEATURE_INTAKE.md`.\n2. Record the classification with `scripts/bin/harness-cli intake`.\n3. Locate the affected product docs and story files.\n4. Check proof status with `scripts/bin/harness-cli query matrix`.\n5. Work only inside the selected lane: tiny, normal, or high-risk.\n6. Before finishing, ask whether product truth, validation expectations,\n   architecture rules, repeated failure patterns, or next-agent instructions\n   changed.\n7. Record a trace with `scripts/bin/harness-cli trace`.\n8. If harness friction was found, either fix it directly or record it with\n   `scripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add`.\n\n## Harness Change Policy\n\nAgents may update directly:\n\n- Story status and evidence via `scripts/bin/harness-cli story update`.\n- Test matrix rows via `scripts/bin/harness-cli story add` and\n  `scripts/bin/harness-cli story update`.\n- Links from story packets to product docs.\n- Validation notes and reports.\n- Small clarifications tied to the current task.\n- Intake records, traces, and backlog items via `scripts/bin/harness-cli`.\n\nAgents should ask for human confirmation before:\n\n- Changing architecture direction.\n- Removing validation requirements.\n- Changing the source-of-truth hierarchy.\n- Changing risk classification rules.\n- Replacing the feature workflow.\n\n## Done Definition\n\nA task is done only when:\n\n- The requested change is completed or the blocker is documented.\n- Relevant docs, stories, and test matrix entries remain current.\n- Validation commands were run when they exist.\n- A trace has been recorded with `scripts/bin/harness-cli trace`.\n- Missing harness capabilities were recorded with\n  `scripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add`.\n- The final response says what changed and what was not attempted.\n\n## Future Validation Ladder\n\nNo validation scripts exist yet. When implementation begins, the expected ladder\nis:\n\n```text\nvalidate:quick\n  format, lint, typecheck, unit tests, architecture check\n\ntest:integration\n  backend, database, provider, or service checks as the stack requires\n\ntest:e2e\n  user-visible end-to-end flows\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260527/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:18:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_7","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API\n\n## Overview\n\nA REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.\n\n## Functional Requirements\n\n### Core Entities\n\n- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps\n- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks\n- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders\n\n### API Endpoints\n\n#### Health\n- `GET /health` → `{ \"status\": \"ok\" }`\n\n#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)\n- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)\n- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)\n- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark\n- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark\n- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark\n\n#### Folders\n- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)\n- `GET /folders` — List all folders\n- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks\n- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name\n- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)\n\n#### Authentication\n- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)\n- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token\n- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented\n\n### Validation Rules\n\n- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL\n- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string\n- Folder `name` must be non-empty string\n- Email must be valid format\n- Password must be >= 8 characters\n\n### Pagination (after T6)\n\n- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`\n- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`\n- Default: page=1, limit=20\n- Max limit: 100\n\n## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)\n\n- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+\n- **Language**: TypeScript\n- **Framework**: Express\n- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3\n- **Testing**: Vitest\n- **Port**: 3000\n\n## Non-Functional Requirements\n\n- All responses are JSON\n- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)\n- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)\n- JWT tokens for session management\n- Database file: `data.db` (application data)\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260527/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:29:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_14","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,320p' src/bookmarks.ts\"","aggregated_output":"import { Router } from \"express\";\nimport type { Database as SqliteDatabase } from \"better-sqlite3\";\nimport type { Request, Response } from \"express\";\n\ntype Bookmark = {\n  id: number;\n  url: string;\n  title: string;\n  description: string | null;\n  folder_id: number | null;\n  created_at: string;\n  updated_at: string;\n};\n\ntype BookmarkInput = {\n  url?: unknown;\n  title?: unknown;\n  description?: unknown;\n  folder_id?: unknown;\n};\n\nfunction sendValidationError(res: Response, message: string): void {\n  res.status(400).json({ error: message });\n}\n\nfunction isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {\n  return typeof value === \"string\" && value.trim().length > 0;\n}\n\nfunction parseBookmarkId(req: Request): number | null {\n  const id = Number(req.params.id);\n  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;\n}\n\nfunction normalizeDescription(value: unknown): string | null {\n  if (value === undefined || value === null) {\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  if (typeof value !== \"string\") {\n    return String(value);\n  }\n\n  return value;\n}\n\nfunction parseOptionalFolderId(value: unknown): number | null | undefined | false {\n  if (value === undefined) {\n    return undefined;\n  }\n\n  if (value === null) {\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  const id = Number(value);\n  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : false;\n}\n\nfunction folderExists(db: SqliteDatabase, folderId: number): boolean {\n  const folder = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT id FROM folders WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(folderId) as { id: number } | undefined;\n\n  return folder !== undefined;\n}\n\nfunction validateFolderId(\n  db: SqliteDatabase,\n  res: Response,\n  value: unknown,\n): number | null | undefined | false {\n  const folderId = parseOptionalFolderId(value);\n\n  if (folderId === false) {\n    sendValidationError(res, \"folder_id must be a positive integer\");\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  if (folderId === undefined || folderId === null) {\n    return folderId;\n  }\n\n  if (!folderExists(db, folderId)) {\n    sendValidationError(res, \"folder_id must reference an existing folder\");\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  return folderId;\n}\n\nexport function createBookmarksRouter(db: SqliteDatabase): Router {\n  const router = Router();\n\n  router.post(\"/\", (req, res) => {\n    const body = req.body as BookmarkInput;\n\n    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url)) {\n      sendValidationError(res, \"url is required\");\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {\n      sendValidationError(res, \"title is required\");\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const folderId = validateFolderId(db, res, body.folder_id);\n\n    if (folderId === false) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const now = new Date().toISOString();\n    const result = db\n      .prepare(\n        `\n        INSERT INTO bookmarks (url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at)\n        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n      `,\n      )\n      .run(\n        body.url.trim(),\n        body.title.trim(),\n        normalizeDescription(body.description),\n        folderId ?? null,\n        now,\n        now,\n      );\n\n    const bookmark = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?\")\n      .get(result.lastInsertRowid) as Bookmark;\n\n    res.status(201).json(bookmark);\n  });\n\n  router.get(\"/\", (_req, res) => {\n    const bookmarks = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM bookmarks ORDER BY id ASC\")\n      .all() as Bookmark[];\n\n    res.status(200).json(bookmarks);\n  });\n\n  router.get(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n    const id = parseBookmarkId(req);\n\n    if (id === null) {\n      res.status(404).json({ error: \"bookmark not found\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const bookmark = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?\")\n      .get(id) as Bookmark | undefined;\n\n    if (!bookmark) {\n      res.status(404).json({ error: \"bookmark not found\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    res.status(200).json(bookmark);\n  });\n\n  router.put(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n    const id = parseBookmarkId(req);\n\n    if (id === null) {\n      res.status(404).json({ error: \"bookmark not found\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const existing = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?\")\n      .get(id) as Bookmark | undefined;\n\n    if (!existing) {\n      res.status(404).json({ error: \"bookmark not found\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const body = req.body as BookmarkInput;\n\n    if (body.url !== undefined && !isNonEmptyString(body.url)) {\n      sendValidationError(res, \"url must be a non-empty string\");\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (body.title !== undefined && !isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {\n      sendValidationError(res, \"title must be a non-empty string\");\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();\n    const nextUrl = body.url === undefined ? existing.url : body.url.trim();\n    const nextTitle =\n      body.title === undefined ? existing.title : body.title.trim();\n    const nextDescription =\n      body.description === undefined\n        ? existing.description\n        : normalizeDescription(body.description);\n    const folderId = validateFolderId(db, res, body.folder_id);\n\n    if (folderId === false) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const nextFolderId =\n      body.folder_id === undefined ? existing.folder_id : folderId;\n\n    db.prepare(\n      `\n      UPDATE bookmarks\n      SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, folder_id = ?, updated_at = ?\n      WHERE id = ?\n    `,\n    ).run(nextUrl, nextTitle, nextDescription, nextFolderId, updatedAt, id);\n\n    const bookmark = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?\")\n      .get(id) as Bookmark;\n\n    res.status(200).json(bookmark);\n  });\n\n  router.delete(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n    const id = parseBookmarkId(req);\n\n    if (id === null) {\n      res.status(404).json({ error: \"bookmark not found\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const result = db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?\").run(id);\n\n    if (result.changes === 0) {\n      res.status(404).json({ error: \"bookmark not found\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    res.status(204).send();\n  });\n\n  return router;\n}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260527/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:31:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_15","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' src/index.ts\"","aggregated_output":"// Bookmark Manager API - entrypoint\n// See PRODUCT_SPEC.md for full requirements\n// The agent will build this out across tasks T1-T6\n\nimport express from \"express\";\nimport { createBookmarksRouter } from \"./bookmarks\";\nimport { db, initializeDatabase } from \"./database\";\nimport { createFoldersRouter } from \"./folders\";\n\nconst app = express();\nconst PORT = Number(process.env.PORT ?? 3000);\n\napp.use(express.json());\n\napp.get(\"/health\", (_req, res) => {\n  res.status(200).json({ status: \"ok\" });\n});\n\ninitializeDatabase();\n\napp.use(\"/bookmarks\", createBookmarksRouter(db));\napp.use(\"/folders\", createFoldersRouter(db));\n\nif (require.main === module) {\n  app.listen(PORT, () => {\n    db.prepare(\"SELECT 1\").get();\n    console.log(`Server running on port ${PORT}`);\n  });\n}\n\nexport function startServer(port: number = PORT) {\n  return app.listen(port, () => {\n    db.prepare(\"SELECT 1\").get();\n    console.log(`Server running on port ${port}`);\n  });\n}\n\nexport default app;\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260527/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:49:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_24","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' docs/stories/T3-folder-support.md\"","aggregated_output":"# T3 Folder Support\n\n## Status\n\nimplemented\n\n## Lane\n\nnormal\n\n## Product Contract\n\nThe Bookmark Manager API persists folders in SQLite `data.db`. Bookmarks can\noptionally reference a folder by `folder_id`, and deleting a folder makes its\nbookmarks unfoldered without deleting the bookmarks.\n\n## Relevant Product Docs\n\n- `PRODUCT_SPEC.md`\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n\n- `POST /folders` creates a folder with required `name` and timestamp fields.\n- `GET /folders` returns all folders as a JSON array.\n- `GET /folders/:id` returns a folder plus its bookmarks or 404.\n- `PUT /folders/:id` updates a folder name or returns 404.\n- `DELETE /folders/:id` deletes a folder with 204 or returns 404.\n- Bookmarks can be created or updated with optional `folder_id`.\n- Non-existent bookmark `folder_id` values return 400.\n- Deleting a folder nullifies matching bookmark `folder_id` values.\n- Existing bookmark endpoints still pass regression tests.\n\n## Design Notes\n\n- Commands: create, update, and delete folders; create and update bookmarks with\n  optional folder assignment.\n- Queries: list folders, read one folder with bookmarks, list/read bookmarks.\n- API: Express router mounted at `/folders`; bookmark router validates\n  `folder_id`.\n- Tables: `folders(id, name, created_at, updated_at)` and nullable\n  `bookmarks.folder_id` referencing `folders(id)`.\n- Domain rules: folder `name` must be a non-empty string; bookmark `folder_id`\n  must be null/omitted or reference an existing folder.\n- UI surfaces: none.\n\n## Validation\n\n| Layer | Expected proof |\n| --- | --- |\n| Unit | Not required for this API slice |\n| Integration | Vitest exercises folder CRUD, foldered bookmarks, nullification, validation, and bookmark regression |\n| E2E | Not required for API-only slice |\n| Platform | `npm run build`; local curl checks; SQLite schema check |\n| Release | Not required |\n\n## Harness Delta\n\nNo harness changes needed.\n\n## Evidence\n\n- `npm run build` succeeded.\n- `npm test` passed 7 integration tests covering bookmark regression and folder\n  support.\n- Manual curl checks against `PORT=3103 node dist/index.js` verified:\n  - `POST /folders` returned 201.\n  - `POST /bookmarks` with `folder_id` returned 201.\n  - `GET /folders/:id` returned the folder and its bookmark.\n  - `DELETE /folders/:id` returned 204.\n  - `GET /bookmarks/:id` after folder deletion showed `folder_id: null`.\n  - `POST /bookmarks` with missing `folder_id` returned 400.\n- `sqlite3 data.db '.schema folders'` showed the required folders table.\n- `sqlite3 data.db '.schema bookmarks'` showed the nullable `folder_id`\n  foreign key on bookmarks.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

id : null;\n}\n\nfunction getAuthenticatedUserId(req: Request, res: Response): number | null {\n  if (typeof req.userId !== \"number\") {\n    res.status(401).json({ error: \"missing or invalid token\" });\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  return req.userId;\n}\n\nexport function createFoldersRouter(db: SqliteDatabase): Router {\n  const router = Router();\n\n  router.post(\"/\", (req, res) => {\n    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req, res);\n\n    if (userId === null) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const body = req.body as FolderInput;\n\n    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {\n      sendValidationError(res, \"name is required\");\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const now = new Date().toISOString();\n    const result = db\n      .prepare(\n        `\n        INSERT INTO folders (user_id, name, created_at, updated_at)\n        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\n      `,\n      )\n      .run(userId, body.name.trim(), now, now);\n\n    const folder = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM folders WHERE id = ?\")\n      .get(result.lastInsertRowid) as Folder;\n\n    res.status(201).json(folder);\n  });\n\n  router.get(\"/\", (req, res) => {\n    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req, res);\n\n    if (userId === null) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const folders = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM folders WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC\")\n      .all(userId) as Folder[];\n\n    res.status(200).json(folders);\n  });\n\n  router.get(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req, res);\n\n    if (userId === null) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const id = parseFolderId(req);\n\n    if (id === null) {\n      res.status(404).json({ error: \"folder not found\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const folder = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n      .get(id, userId) as Folder | undefined;\n\n    if (!folder) {\n      res.status(404).json({ error: \"folder not found\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const bookmarks = db\n      .prepare(\n        \"SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE folder_id = ? AND user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC\",\n      )\n      .all(id, userId) as Bookmark[];\n\n    res.status(200).json({ ...folder, bookmarks });\n  });\n\n  router.put(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req, res);\n\n    if (userId === null) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const id = parseFolderId(req);\n\n    if (id === null) {\n      res.status(404).json({ error: \"folder not found\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const existing = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n      .get(id, userId) as Folder | undefined;\n\n    if (!existing) {\n      res.status(404).json({ error: \"folder not found\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const body = req.body as FolderInput;\n\n    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {\n      sendValidationError(res, \"name is required\");\n      return;\n    }\n\n    db.prepare(\n      `\n      UPDATE folders\n      SET name = ?, updated_at = ?\n      WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\n    `,\n    ).run(body.name.trim(), new Date().toISOString(), id, userId);\n\n    const folder = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n      .get(id, userId) as Folder;\n\n    res.status(200).json(folder);\n  });\n\n  router.delete(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req, res);\n\n    if (userId === null) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const id = parseFolderId(req);\n\n    if (id === null) {\n      res.status(404).json({ error: \"folder not found\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const existing = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT id FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n      .get(id, userId) as { id: number } | undefined;\n\n    if (!existing) {\n      res.status(404).json({ error: \"folder not found\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const deleteFolder = db.transaction(() => {\n      db.prepare(\"UPDATE bookmarks SET folder_id = NULL WHERE folder_id = ?\").run(\n        id,\n      );\n      db.prepare(\n        \"UPDATE bookmarks SET folder_id = NULL WHERE folder_id = ? AND user_id = ?\",\n      ).run(id, userId);\n      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\").run(\n        id,\n        userId,\n      );\n    });\n\n    deleteFolder();\n\n    res.status(204).send();\n  });\n\n  return router;\n}\nimport { Router } from \"express\";\nimport type { Database as SqliteDatabase } from \"better-sqlite3\";\nimport type { Request, Response } from \"express\";\n\ntype Bookmark = {\n  id: number;\n  user_id: number;\n  url: string;\n  title: string;\n  description: string | null;\n  folder_id: number | null;\n  created_at: string;\n  updated_at: string;\n};\n\ntype BookmarkInput = {\n  url?: unknown;\n  title?: unknown;\n  description?: unknown;\n  folder_id?: unknown;\n};\n\nfunction sendValidationError(res: Response, message: string): void {\n  res.status(400).json({ error: message });\n}\n\nfunction isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {\n  return typeof value === \"string\" && value.trim().length > 0;\n}\n\nfunction parseBookmarkId(req: Request): number | null {\n  const id = Number(req.params.id);\n  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;\n}\n\nfunction getAuthenticatedUserId(req: Request, res: Response): number | null {\n  if (typeof req.userId !== \"number\") {\n    res.status(401).json({ error: \"missing or invalid token\" });\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  return req.userId;\n}\n\nfunction normalizeDescription(value: unknown): string | null {\n  if (value === undefined || value === null) {\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  if (typeof value !== \"string\") {\n    return String(value);\n  }\n\n  return value;\n}\n\nfunction parseOptionalFolderId(value: unknown): number | null | undefined | false {\n  if (value === undefined) {\n    return undefined;\n  }\n\n  if (value === null) {\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  const id = Number(value);\n  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : false;\n}\n\nfunction folderExists(\n  db: SqliteDatabase,\n  userId: number,\n  folderId: number,\n): boolean {\n  const folder = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT id FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n    .get(folderId, userId) as { id: number } | undefined;\n\n  return folder !== undefined;\n}\n\nfunction validateFolderId(\n  db: SqliteDatabase,\n  userId: number,\n  res: Response,\n  value: unknown,\n): number | null | undefined | false {\n  const folderId = parseOptionalFolderId(value);\n\n  if (folderId === false) {\n    sendValidationError(res, \"folder_id must be a positive integer\");\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  if (folderId === undefined || folderId === null) {\n    return folderId;\n  }\n\n  if (!folderExists(db, userId, folderId)) {\n    sendValidationError(res, \"folder_id must reference an existing folder\");\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  return folderId;\n}\n\nexport function createBookmarksRouter(db: SqliteDatabase): Router {\n  const router = Router();\n\n  router.post(\"/\", (req, res) => {\n    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req, res);\n\n    if (userId === null) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const body = req.body as BookmarkInput;\n\n    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url)) {\n      sendValidationError(res, \"url is required\");\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {\n      sendValidationError(res, \"title is required\");\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const folderId = validateFolderId(db, userId, res, body.folder_id);\n\n    if (folderId === false) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const now = new Date().toISOString();\n    const result = db\n      .prepare(\n        `\n        INSERT INTO bookmarks (user_id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at)\n        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n      `,\n      )\n      .run(\n        userId,\n        body.url.trim(),\n        body.title.trim(),\n        normalizeDescription(body.description),\n        folderId ?? null,\n        now,\n        now,\n      );\n\n    const bookmark = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?\")\n      .get(result.lastInsertRowid) as Bookmark;\n\n    res.status(201).json(bookmark);\n  });\n\n  router.get(\"/\", (req, res) => {\n    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req, res);\n\n    if (userId === null) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const bookmarks = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC\")\n      .all(userId) as Bookmark[];\n\n    res.status(200).json(bookmarks);\n  });\n\n  router.get(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req, res);\n\n    if (userId === null) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const id = parseBookmarkId(req);\n\n    if (id === null) {\n      res.status(404).json({ error: \"bookmark not found\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const bookmark = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n      .get(id, userId) as Bookmark | undefined;\n\n    if (!bookmark) {\n      res.status(404).json({ error: \"bookmark not found\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    res.status(200).json(bookmark);\n  });\n\n  router.put(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req, res);\n\n    if (userId === null) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const id = parseBookmarkId(req);\n\n    if (id === null) {\n      res.status(404).json({ error: \"bookmark not found\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const existing = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n      .get(id, userId) as Bookmark | undefined;\n\n    if (!existing) {\n      res.status(404).json({ error: \"bookmark not found\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const body = req.body as BookmarkInput;\n\n    if (body.url !== undefined && !isNonEmptyString(body.url)) {\n      sendValidationError(res, \"url must be a non-empty string\");\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (body.title !== undefined && !isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {\n      sendValidationError(res, \"title must be a non-empty string\");\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();\n    const nextUrl = body.url === undefined ? existing.url : body.url.trim();\n    const nextTitle =\n      body.title === undefined ? existing.title : body.title.trim();\n    const nextDescription =\n      body.description === undefined\n        ? existing.description\n        : normalizeDescription(body.description);\n    const folderId = validateFolderId(db, userId, res, body.folder_id);\n\n    if (folderId === false) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const nextFolderId =\n      body.folder_id === undefined ? existing.folder_id : folderId;\n\n    db.prepare(\n      `\n      UPDATE bookmarks\n      SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, folder_id = ?, updated_at = ?\n      WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\n    `,\n    ).run(\n      nextUrl,\n      nextTitle,\n      nextDescription,\n      nextFolderId,\n      updatedAt,\n      id,\n      userId,\n    );\n\n    const bookmark = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n      .get(id, userId) as Bookmark;\n\n    res.status(200).json(bookmark);\n  });\n\n  router.delete(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req, res);\n\n    if (userId === null) {\n      return;\n    }\n// Bookmark Manager API - entrypoint\n// See PRODUCT_SPEC.md for full requirements\n// The agent will build this out across tasks T1-T6\n\nimport express from \"express\";\nimport { createBookmarksRouter } from \"./bookmarks\";\nimport { db, initializeDatabase } from \"./database\";\nimport { createFoldersRouter } from \"./folders\";\n\nconst app = express();\nconst PORT = Number(process.env.PORT ?? 3000);\n\napp.use(express.json());\n\napp.get(\"/health\", (_req, res) => {\n  res.status(200).json({ status: \"ok\" });\n});\n\ninitializeDatabase();\n\napp.use(\"/bookmarks\", createBookmarksRouter(db));\napp.use(\"/folders\", createFoldersRouter(db));\n\nif (require.main === module) {\n  app.listen(PORT, () => {\n    db.prepare(\"SELECT 1\").get();\n    console.log(`Server running on port ${PORT}`);\n  });\n}\n\nexport function startServer(port: number = PORT) {\n  return app.listen(port, () => {\n    db.prepare(\"SELECT 1\").get();\n    console.log(`Server running on port ${port}`);\n  });\n}\n\nexport default app;\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260527/T6-pagination/events.jsonl:12:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_6","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API\n\n## Overview\n\nA REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.\n\n## Functional Requirements\n\n### Core Entities\n\n- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps\n- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks\n- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders\n\n### API Endpoints\n\n#### Health\n- `GET /health` → `{ \"status\": \"ok\" }`\n\n#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)\n- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)\n- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)\n- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark\n- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark\n- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark\n\n#### Folders\n- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)\n- `GET /folders` — List all folders\n- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks\n- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name\n- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)\n\n#### Authentication\n- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)\n- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token\n- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented\n\n### Validation Rules\n\n- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL\n- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string\n- Folder `name` must be non-empty string\n- Email must be valid format\n- Password must be >= 8 characters\n\n### Pagination (after T6)\n\n- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`\n- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`\n- Default: page=1, limit=20\n- Max limit: 100\n\n## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)\n\n- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+\n- **Language**: TypeScript\n- **Framework**: Express\n- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3\n- **Testing**: Vitest\n- **Port**: 3000\n\n## Non-Functional Requirements\n\n- All responses are JSON\n- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)\n- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)\n- JWT tokens for session management\n- Database file: `data.db` (application data)\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260527/T6-pagination/events.jsonl:13:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_4","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' docs/ARCHITECTURE.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Architecture\n\nNo application stack is selected yet.\n\nNo application code exists yet. This document defines generic architecture\nquestions and boundary rules that future implementation should adapt after a\nuser-provided spec and stack decision exist.\n\n## Discovery Before Shape\n\nBefore proposing implementation shape, identify:\n\n- Product surfaces: browser, mobile, desktop, CLI, API, worker, or service.\n- Runtime stack: language, framework, database, queues, providers, and hosting.\n- Core domains: the product concepts that deserve stable names and contracts.\n- Boundary inputs: user input, API requests, webhooks, jobs, files, credentials,\n  provider payloads, and environment configuration.\n- Validation ladder: the smallest checks that can prove the selected stack.\n\nRecord stack choices in `docs/decisions/` when they meaningfully constrain\nfuture work.\n\n## Default Layering\n\n```text\ndomain\n  <- application\n      <- infrastructure\n          <- interface\n              <- app surfaces\n```\n\n## Candidate Structure\n\n```text\napp/\n  domain/\n    entities/\n    value-objects/\n    repositories/\n    services/\n\n  application/\n    commands/\n    queries/\n    handlers/\n\n  infrastructure/\n    database/\n    logging/\n    notifications/\n\n  interface/\n    controllers/\n    dto/\n    presenters/\n    routes/\n    middlewares/\n\nsurfaces/\n  browser/\n  mobile/\n  desktop/\n  cli/\n```\n\nThis is a thinking template, not a scaffold. Create real folders only when a\nstory enters implementation and the selected stack needs them.\n\n## Dependency Rule\n\nInner layers must not depend on outer layers.\n\n| Layer | May depend on | Must not depend on |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| domain | nothing project-external except tiny pure utilities | framework, database, UI, provider, process/env |\n| application | domain | framework, UI, provider, database concrete clients |\n| infrastructure | domain, application | interface controllers or UI |\n| interface | all backend layers | UI state or platform shell assumptions |\n| app surfaces | API contracts and app-facing clients | domain internals directly |\n\n## Parse-First Boundary Rule\n\nUnknown data must be parsed at boundaries before it enters inner code.\n\nBoundaries include:\n\n- HTTP request bodies, params, and query strings.\n- Session payloads and identity claims.\n- Environment variables.\n- Database rows returned from external clients.\n- Platform shell payloads.\n- Deep links, tokens, and signed URLs.\n- Provider webhooks, events, and async payloads.\n\nTarget flow:\n\n```text\nunknown input\n  -> parser\n  -> typed DTO or command\n  -> application use case\n  -> domain object/value object\n```\n\nInner layers should work with meaningful product types such as `UserId`,\n`AccountId`, `WorkspaceId`, `Role`, `DateRange`, or domain-specific IDs,\nrather than repeatedly validating raw strings.\n\n## Command/Query Boundary\n\nIf the product has both reads and writes, keep command/query separation clear at\nthe code level even when the storage layer is simple:\n\n- Commands mutate state and own audit side effects.\n- Queries read state and format for consumers.\n- Shared domain rules live in domain/application, not controllers.\n\n## Observability Contract\n\nThe future server should emit one canonical JSON log line per request with:\n\n- timestamp\n- level\n- request_id\n- user_id when known\n- action\n- duration_ms\n- status_code\n- message\n\nAudit logs are product records. ./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260527/T6-pagination/events.jsonl:16:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_7","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' docs/HARNESS.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Harness\n\nThe project goal is to provide a reusable operating harness that lets humans and\nagents turn a future product spec into safe, validated work.\n\nThe app is what users touch. The harness is what agents touch.\n\n## Mental Model\n\n```text\n------------------+\n| Human intent    |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Feature intake   |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Story packet     |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Agent work loop  |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Product delta    |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Validation proof |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Harness delta    |\n+------------------+\n         |\n         v\n+------------------+\n| Next intent      |\n+------------------+\n```\n\nEvery task has two possible outputs:\n\n1. Product delta: app code, tests, API shape, data model, or product docs.\n2. Harness delta: docs, templates, validation expectations, backlog items, or\n   decision records that make the next task easier.\n\n## Harness v0 Scope\n\nHarness v0 includes:\n\n- Agent entrypoint.\n- Empty product documentation structure.\n- Feature intake and risk lanes.\n- Story templates.\n- Decision log template.\n- Validation report template.\n- Test matrix placeholder.\n- Harness growth backlog.\n- Durable layer: SQLite database and CLI for operational records.\n\nHarness v0 deliberately excludes:\n\n- A project-specific `SPEC.md`.\n- Pre-sliced product domains.\n- A locked application stack.\n- App source scaffolding.\n- Package scripts.\n- Test runner config.\n- CI workflows.\n\nThose should arrive only when a selected story needs them.\n\n## Durable Layer\n\nPolicy documents describe how to work. The durable layer stores what happened.\n\nOperational data — intake classifications, story status, decision outcomes,\nbacklog items, and execution traces — lives in a SQLite database (`harness.db`)\nmanaged by the Rust Harness CLI at `scripts/bin/harness-cli`. Agents and humans\nshould use that binary for Harness work. The database is local to each project\ninstance and `.gitignore`d. The schema is version-controlled under\n`scripts/schema/`.\n\nThis separation keeps policy docs stable and human-readable while giving agents\na structured, queryable record of operational state. It also prepares the\nharness for future observability and automated evolution without adding more\nmarkdown files.\n\nInitialize the database if it does not exist:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli init\n```\n\nCommon commands:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli intake  --type <type> --summary <text> --lane <lane>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli story   add --id <id> --title <text> --lane <lane>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli story   update --id <id> --status <status>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli trace   --summary <text> --outcome <outcome>\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query   matrix\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query   backlog\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query   stats\n```\n\n## Source Hierarchy\n\n```text\nUser-provided spec or prompt\n  input material for first buildout or future changes\n\ndocs/product/*\n  current product contract derived from accepted input\n\ndocs/stories/*\n  story-sized work packets and historical evidence\n\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query matrix\n  behavior-to-proof control panel backed by the durable layer\n\ndocs/decisions/*\n  why the contract changed\n```\n\nBefore implementation, product docs describe intent. After implementation,\nproduct docs plus executable tests become the living contract.\n\n## Spec Lifecycle\n\nHarness v0 starts without a tracked project spec. When the human provides a\nspecification, treat it as input material, not as a permanent operating manual.\nUse it to populate product docs, story packets, architecture decisions, and\nvalidation expectations during the first buildout.\n\nAfter the specification has been decomposed, do not keep extending it as the\nliving product plan. Ongoing work should update the smaller product docs,\nstories, durable proof records, and decision records.\n\nOngoing work should enter the harness as one of these input types:\n\n- New spec: a project specification that needs to become product docs and\n  initial story candidates.\n- Spec slice: a selected behavior from the provided spec.\n- Change request: a bounded behavior change, bug fix, or product refinement.\n- New initiative: a larger product area that needs multiple stories.\n- Maintenance request: dependency, architecture, performance, security, or\n  operational work.\n- Harness improvement: a process, template, proof, or agent-instruction change.\n\nThe spec-to-work loop is:\n\n```text\nhuman intent or supplied spec\n  -> classify input type\n  -> update or create product contract\n  -> create story packet or initiative notes when needed\n  -> define validation proof\n  -> implement or document the blocker\n  -> update product docs, stories, durable proof records, and decisions\n  -> capture harness friction\n```\n\nLarge product areas should use scoped initiative notes instead of a second\nmonolithic specification. An initiative should explain the goal, affected\nproduct docs, candidate stories, validation shape, open decisions, and exit\ncriteria. If initiative work becomes a repeated pattern, add a template or\nrecord the proposal with `scripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add`.\n\n## Growth Rule\n\nThe harness grows from friction.\n\nWhen an agent is confused, repeats manual reasoning, needs a new validation\ncommand, discovers a missing rule, or sees a recurring failure pattern, it must\neither improve the harness directly or record the friction:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add --title \"<short name>\" --pain \"<what was hard>\"\n```\n\nThe `harness_friction` field on traces also captures per-task friction so\npatterns can be queried later:\n\n```bash\nscripts/bin/harness-cli query friction\n```\n\n## Task Loop\n\nFor every task:\n\n1. Classify the request with `docs/FEATURE_INTAKE.md`.\n2. Record the classification with `scripts/bin/harness-cli intake`.\n3. Locate the affected product docs and story files.\n4. Check proof status with `scripts/bin/harness-cli query matrix`.\n5. Work only inside the selected lane: tiny, normal, or high-risk.\n6. Before finishing, ask whether product truth, validation expectations,\n   architecture rules, repeated failure patterns, or next-agent instructions\n   changed.\n7. Record a trace with `scripts/bin/harness-cli trace`.\n8. If harness friction was found, either fix it directly or record it with\n   `scripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add`.\n\n## Harness Change Policy\n\nAgents may update directly:\n\n- Story status and evidence via `scripts/bin/harness-cli story update`.\n- Test matrix rows via `scripts/bin/harness-cli story add` and\n  `scripts/bin/harness-cli story update`.\n- Links from story packets to product docs.\n- Validation notes and reports.\n- Small clarifications tied to the current task.\n- Intake records, traces, and backlog items via `scripts/bin/harness-cli`.\n\nAgents should ask for human confirmation before:\n\n- Changing architecture direction.\n- Removing validation requirements.\n- Changing the source-of-truth hierarchy.\n- Changing risk classification rules.\n- Replacing the feature workflow.\n\n## Done Definition\n\nA task is done only when:\n\n- The requested change is completed or the blocker is documented.\n- Relevant docs, stories, and test matrix entries remain current.\n- Validation commands were run when they exist.\n- A trace has been recorded with `scripts/bin/harness-cli trace`.\n- Missing harness capabilities were recorded with\n  `scripts/bin/harness-cli backlog add`.\n- The final response says what changed and what was not attempted.\n\n## Future Validation Ladder\n\nNo validation scripts exist yet. When implementation begins, the expected ladder\nis:\n\n```text\nvalidate:quick\n  format, lint, typecheck, unit tests, architecture check\n\ntest:integration\n  backend, database, provider, or service checks as the stack requires\n\ntest:e2e\n  user-visible end-to-end flows\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

id : null;\n}\n\nfunction getAuthenticatedUserId(req: Request, res: Response): number | null {\n  if (typeof req.userId !== \"number\") {\n    res.status(401).json({ error: \"missing or invalid token\" });\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  return req.userId;\n}\n\nfunction normalizeDescription(value: unknown): string | null {\n  if (value === undefined || value === null) {\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  if (typeof value !== \"string\") {\n    return String(value);\n  }\n\n  return value;\n}\n\nfunction parseOptionalFolderId(value: unknown): number | null | undefined | false {\n  if (value === undefined) {\n    return undefined;\n  }\n\n  if (value === null) {\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  const id = Number(value);\n  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : false;\n}\n\nfunction folderExists(\n  db: SqliteDatabase,\n  userId: number,\n  folderId: number,\n): boolean {\n  const folder = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT id FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n    .get(folderId, userId) as { id: number } | undefined;\n\n  return folder !== undefined;\n}\n\nfunction validateFolderId(\n  db: SqliteDatabase,\n  userId: number,\n  res: Response,\n  value: unknown,\n): number | null | undefined | false {\n  const folderId = parseOptionalFolderId(value);\n\n  if (folderId === false) {\n    sendValidationError(res, \"folder_id must be a positive integer\");\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  if (folderId === undefined || folderId === null) {\n    return folderId;\n  }\n\n  if (!folderExists(db, userId, folderId)) {\n    sendValidationError(res, \"folder_id must reference an existing folder\");\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  return folderId;\n}\n\nexport function createBookmarksRouter(db: SqliteDatabase): Router {\n  const router = Router();\n\n  router.post(\"/\", (req, res) => {\n    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req, res);\n\n    if (userId === null) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const body = req.body as BookmarkInput;\n\n    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url)) {\n      sendValidationError(res, \"url must be a non-empty string\");\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {\n      sendValidationError(res, \"title must be a non-empty string\");\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const folderId = validateFolderId(db, userId, res, body.folder_id);\n\n    if (folderId === false) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const now = new Date().toISOString();\n    const result = db\n      .prepare(\n        `\n        INSERT INTO bookmarks (user_id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at)\n        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n      `,\n      )\n      .run(\n        userId,\n        body.url.trim(),\n        body.title.trim(),\n        normalizeDescription(body.description),\n        folderId ?? null,\n        now,\n        now,\n      );\n\n    const bookmark = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?\")\n      .get(result.lastInsertRowid) as Bookmark;\n\n    res.status(201).json(bookmark);\n  });\n\n  router.get(\"/\", (req, res) => {\n    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req, res);\n\n    if (userId === null) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const bookmarks = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC\")\n      .all(userId) as Bookmark[];\n\n    res.status(200).json(bookmarks);\n  });\n\n  router.get(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req, res);\n\n    if (userId === null) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const id = parseBookmarkId(req);\n\n    if (id === null) {\n      res.status(404).json({ error: \"bookmark not found\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const bookmark = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n      .get(id, userId) as Bookmark | undefined;\n\n    if (!existing) {\n      res.status(404).json({ error: \"bookmark not found\" });\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const body = req.body as BookmarkInput;\n\n    if (body.url !== undefined && !isNonEmptyString(body.url)) {\n      sendValidationError(res, \"url must be a non-empty string\");\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (body.title !== undefined && !isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {\n      sendValidationError(res, \"title must be a non-empty string\");\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();\n    const nextUrl = body.url === undefined ? existing.url : body.url.trim();\n    const nextTitle =\n      body.title === undefined ? existing.title : body.title.trim();\n    const nextDescription =\n      body.description === undefined\n        ? existing.description\n        : normalizeDescription(body.description);\n    const folderId = validateFolderId(db, userId, res, body.folder_id);\n\n    if (folderId === false) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const nextFolderId =\n      body.folder_id === undefined ? existing.folder_id : folderId;\n\n    db.prepare(\n      `\n      UPDATE bookmarks\n      SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, folder_id = ?, updated_at = ?\n      WHERE id = ? ./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260527/T6-pagination/events.jsonl:38:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_19","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' src/index.ts\"","aggregated_output":"// Bookmark Manager API - entrypoint\n// See PRODUCT_SPEC.md for full requirements\n// The agent will build this out across tasks T1-T6\n\nimport express from \"express\";\nimport { authenticateToken, createAuthRouter } from \"./auth\";\nimport { createBookmarksRouter } from \"./bookmarks\";\nimport { db, initializeDatabase } from \"./database\";\nimport { createFoldersRouter } from \"./folders\";\n\nconst app = express();\nconst PORT = Number(process.env.PORT ?? 3000);\n\napp.use(express.json());\n\napp.get(\"/health\", (_req, res) => {\n  res.status(200).json({ status: \"ok\" });\n});\n\ninitializeDatabase();\n\napp.use(\"/auth\", createAuthRouter(db));\napp.use(\"/bookmarks\", authenticateToken(), createBookmarksRouter(db));\napp.use(\"/folders\", authenticateToken(), createFoldersRouter(db));\n\nif (require.main === module) {\n  app.listen(PORT, () => {\n    db.prepare(\"SELECT 1\").get();\n    console.log(`Server running on port ${PORT}`);\n  });\n}\n\nexport function startServer(port: number = PORT) {\n  return app.listen(port, () => {\n    db.prepare(\"SELECT 1\").get();\n    console.log(`Server running on port ${port}`);\n  });\n}\n\nexport default app;\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-20260527/T6-pagination/events.jsonl:41:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_20","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,260p' docs/product/bookmark-api.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Bookmark API\n\n## Ownership\n\nBookmarks and folders are owned by the authenticated user. Users can only list,\nread, create, update, or delete records that belong to them.\n\n## Bookmarks\n\n- `POST /bookmarks` creates a bookmark for the authenticated user.\n- `GET /bookmarks` lists only the authenticated user's bookmarks.\n- `GET /bookmarks/:id` returns `404` when the bookmark does not exist or belongs\n  to a different user.\n- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` updates only the authenticated user's bookmark.\n- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` deletes only the authenticated user's bookmark.\n- `folder_id`, when provided, must reference one of the authenticated user's\n  folders.\n\n## Folders\n\n- `POST /folders` creates a folder for the authenticated user.\n- `GET /folders` lists only the authenticated user's folders.\n- `GET /folders/:id` returns the folder plus only that user's bookmarks in the\n  folder.\n- `GET /folders/:id` returns `404` when the folder does not exist or belongs to\n  a different user.\n- `PUT /folders/:id` updates only the authenticated user's folder.\n- `DELETE /folders/:id` deletes only the authenticated user's folder and\n  unfolders only that user's matching bookmarks.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Read `PRODUCT_SPEC.md` for the full specification.\n\n## Task\n\nRefactor the `GET /bookmarks` endpoint to support pagination:\n\n1. Change the response format from a plain array to a paginated envelope:\n   ```json\n   {\n     \"data\": [...],\n     \"page\": 1,\n     \"limit\": 20,\n     \"total\": 45\n   }\n   ```\n2. Accept query parameters:\n   - `page` (integer, default: 1, minimum: 1)\n   - `limit` (integer, default: 20, minimum: 1, maximum: 100)\n3. Implement proper SQL OFFSET/LIMIT pagination\n4. Return correct `total` count (total bookmarks for this user, not just current page)\n5. Validate query parameters:\n   - `page=0` or `page=-1` → 400\n   - `limit=0` or `limit=200` → 400\n   - Non-numeric values → 400 or use defaults\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n\n- `GET /bookmarks` returns paginated envelope (not raw array)\n- Default pagination: page=1, limit=20\n- `GET /bookmarks?page=2&limit=5` returns correct offset\n- `total` reflects actual total count, not page count\n- Invalid page/limit values → 400\n- All other endpoints still work (including auth)\n- Response includes all expected fields: `data`, `page`, `limit`, `total`\n\n## Notes\n\n- This is a refactoring task — the endpoint behavior changes for ALL callers\n- The `GET /folders/:id` endpoint (which returns bookmarks) does NOT need pagination yet\n- Consider: should the `GET /folders` endpoint also be paginated? (No — keep it simple)\n- SQL: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bookmarks WHERE user_id = ?` for total\n- SQL: `SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE user_id = ? LIMIT ? Users can only list,\n     6\tread, create, update, or delete records that belong to them.\n     7\t\n     8\t## Bookmarks\n     9\t\n    10\t- `POST /bookmarks` creates a bookmark for the authenticated user.\n    11\t- `GET /bookmarks` lists only the authenticated user's bookmarks in a\n    12\t  paginated envelope: `{ data, page, limit, total }`.\n    13\t- `GET /bookmarks` accepts optional `page` and `limit` query parameters.\n    14\t  Defaults are `page=1` and `limit=20`; `page` must be at least 1 and `limit`\n    15\t  must be between 1 and 100.\n    16\t- `GET /bookmarks` returns `total` as the authenticated user's full bookmark\n    17\t  count, not just the current page size.\n    18\t- `GET /bookmarks/:id` returns `404` when the bookmark does not exist or belongs\n    19\t  to a different user.\n    20\t- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` updates only the authenticated user's bookmark.\n    21\t- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` deletes only the authenticated user's bookmark.\n    22\t- `folder_id`, when provided, must reference one of the authenticated user's\n    23\t  folders.\n    24\t\n    25\t## Folders\n    26\t\n    27\t- `POST /folders` creates a folder for the authenticated user.\n    28\t- `GET /folders` lists only the authenticated user's folders.\n    29\t- `GET /folders/:id` returns the folder plus only that user's bookmarks in the\n    30\t  folder.\n    31\t- `GET /folders/:id` returns `404` when the folder does not exist or belongs to\n    32\t  a different user.\n    33\t- `PUT /folders/:id` updates only the authenticated user's folder.\n    34\t- `DELETE /folders/:id` deletes only the authenticated user's folder and\n    35\t  unfolders only that user's matching bookmarks.\n     1\t# T6 Pagination\n     2\t\n     3\t## Status\n     4\t\n     5\timplemented\n     6\t\n     7\t## Lane\n     8\t\n     9\tnormal\n    10\t\n    11\t## Product Contract\n    12\t\n    13\t`GET /bookmarks` returns a paginated response envelope for the authenticated\n    14\tuser instead of a raw bookmark array. The endpoint validates pagination query\n    15\tparameters at the HTTP boundary and reports the total bookmark count for the\n    16\tuser separately from the current page data.\n    17\t\n    18\t## Relevant Product Docs\n    19\t\n    20\t- `PRODUCT_SPEC.md`\n    21\t- `docs/product/bookmark-api.md`\n    22\t\n    23\t## Acceptance Criteria\n    24\t\n    25\t- `GET /bookmarks` returns `{ data, page, limit, total }`.\n    26\t- Default pagination uses `page=1` and `limit=20`.\n    27\t- `GET /bookmarks?page=2&limit=5` uses SQL offset pagination.\n    28\t- `total` reflects all bookmarks for the authenticated user.\n    29\t- Invalid `page` or `limit` values return 400.\n    30\t- Bookmark authentication and other CRUD endpoints continue to work.\n    31\t\n    32\t## Design Notes\n    33\t\n    34\t- Commands: unchanged.\n    35\t- Queries: bookmark list query now performs a `COUNT(*)` for total and a\n    36\t  paginated `SELECT` with `LIMIT` and `OFFSET`.\n    37\t- API: `GET /bookmarks` response shape is a breaking change for callers that\n    38\t  expected a raw array.\n    39\t- Tables: unchanged.\n    40\t- Domain rules: pagination parameters must be positive integers; `limit` is\n    41\t  capped at 100.\n    42\t- UI surfaces: none.\n    43\t\n    44\t## Validation\n    45\t\n    46\t| Layer | Expected proof |\n    47\t| --- | --- |\n    48\t| Unit | Not used for this Express-only project. |\n    49\t| Integration | `npm test` covers default pagination, page-two offset, total count, invalid query values, auth, and existing CRUD behavior. |\n    50\t| E2E | Not used. |\n    51\t| Platform | `npm run build` proves TypeScript compile. |\n    52\t| Release | Not used. |\n    53\t\n    54\t## Harness Delta\n    55\t\n    56\tNo harness changes required.\n    57\t\n    58\t## Evidence\n    59\t\n    60\t- `npm run build`\n    61\t- `npm test` (14 integration tests)\n    62\t- `benchmark/lib/check-functional.sh` T6 pagination checks passed under the\n    63\t  Node 22 path (9 curl checks)\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

item.aggregated_output:
# Bookmarks

Bookmarks are saved URLs owned by an authenticated user with a required URL,
required title, optional description, optional folder assignment, and ISO
timestamp metadata.

Folders are named collections of bookmarks owned by an authenticated user.
Deleting a folder does not delete its bookmarks; those bookmarks become
unfoldered.

Users authenticate with email and password. Passwords are stored as hashes, and
login returns a JWT bearer token.

## API

- `POST /auth/register` creates a user and returns `201` with `id`, `email`,
  and `created_at`, or `409` when the email already exists.
- `POST /auth/login` authenticates a user and returns `200` with
  `{ "token": string }`, or `401` when credentials are invalid.
- All `/bookmarks` and `/folders` endpoints require
  `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return `401` when the token is missing or
  invalid.
- `POST /bookmarks` creates a bookmark for the authenticated user and returns
  `201` with the created row.
- `GET /bookmarks` returns `200` with the authenticated user's bookmarks as an
  array.
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` returns `200` with one bookmark, or `404` when absent.
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` updates an existing bookmark and returns `200` with the
  updated row, or `404` when absent.
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` deletes an existing bookmark and returns `204`, or
  `404` when absent.
- `POST /folders` creates a folder for the authenticated user and returns `201`
  with the created row.
- `GET /folders` returns `200` with the authenticated user's folders as an
  array.
- `GET /folders/:id` returns `200` with one folder and its `bookmarks`, or
  `404` when absent.
- `PUT /folders/:id` updates a folder name and returns `200` with the updated
  row, or `404` when absent.
- `DELETE /folders/:id` deletes a folder and returns `204`; bookmarks formerly
  assigned to it remain saved with `folder_id = null`.

## Validation

- Email is required for registration and login and must be a valid email shape.
- Password is required for registration and login and must be at least 8
  characters.
- `url` is required on create.
- `url` must be a valid URL on create and update.
- `title` is required on create and must be a non-empty string.
- Provided `url` and `title` update values must be non-empty strings.
- `folder_id` is optional on bookmark create and update. When provided as a
  number, it must reference an existing folder owned by the authenticated user
  or the API returns `400`.
- `folder_id` may be `null` to leave or make a bookmark unfoldered.
- Folder `name` is required and must be a non-empty string.

## Storage

Bookmarks persist in SQLite `data.db` in a `bookmarks` table with `id`, `url`,
`title`, `description`, `folder_id`, `user_id`, `created_at`, and `updated_at`
columns. Folders persist in a `folders` table with `id`, `name`, `user_id`,
`created_at`, and `updated_at` columns. Users persist in a `users` table with
`id`, unique `email`, `password_hash`, and `created_at` columns.

item.text: I’m making the validation strict for explicit query parameters: absent values use defaults, but malformed values like `abc`, `1.5`, `0`, negative numbers, repeated parameters, or `limit > 100` return `400`. That matches the acceptance criteria without silently hiding bad caller input.
The first patch missed because the SQL block had a slightly different shape than expected. I’ve re-read the exact lines and am applying a narrower patch.

The route now has `COUNT(*)` plus `LIMIT/OFFSET`. I’m updating the integration assertions so auth scoping expects the envelope, then adding a dedicated pagination test that verifies page-two ordering and invalid query values.

The code and docs are patched. I’m running the TypeScript build and test suite now; if tests expose any state-coupling from the existing integration file, I’ll tighten the test setup rather than weakening the assertions.
